label: melanoma
original: Histopathology showing spindle cell uveal melanoma from a left eye choroidal pigmented malignant melanoma, classified as invasive in the ciliary body and cornea (stage IIIB, pT4bN0M0). The microscopic examination reveals a dense cell proliferation composed of small and medium fusiform (spindle) cells, along with evident pigment production. The tissue fragments were embedded in paraffin for this histopathological examination. Staining is Hematoxylin–Eosin (HE), magnified ×100.
cleaned: Dense cell proliferation composed of small and medium fusiform cells with evident pigment production. Uveal melanoma.

label: melanoma
original: Histopathology showing spindle cell uveal melanoma with corneal invasion. The image is stained with hematoxylin and eosin (HE) at ×100 magnification.
cleaned: Spindle cells infiltrating corneal tissue. Lesion demonstrates a melanoma morphology. Diagnosis: uveal melanoma.

label: melanoma
original: Histopathology showing spindle cell uveal melanoma with invasion of the ciliary processes. The image is stained with hematoxylin and eosin (HE) at ×100 magnification.
cleaned: Spindle cells infiltrating ciliary processes. Uveal melanoma.

label: melanoma
original: Histopathology at ×20 magnification, illustrating amelanotic variants of melanoma cells. These cells lack melanin, making them challenging to diagnose in routine hematoxylin and eosin (H&E)-stained specimens and potentially leading to misidentification with non-melanocytic tumors. The scale bar represents 50 μm.
cleaned: Melanoma cells lacking melanin. Cells exhibit varied morphology.

The scale bar represents 50 μm.
cleaned: Melanoma cells lacking melanin. Cells exhibit varied morphology.

original: Histopathology at ×20 magnification, illustrating amelanotic variants of melanoma cells. label: melanoma
original: Histopathology from a skin biopsy of a 35-year-old woman presenting with a pigmented patch on the right breast, demonstrating epidermal acanthosis and hyperpigmentation of basal cells, particularly pronounced at the rete pegs of the epidermis. The biopsy findings also included hyperpigmentation of the basement membrane associated with numerous melanophages, with no cytonuclear atypia or melanocytic hyperplasia, which helped to exclude cutaneous melanoma and pigmented Paget's disease. This image supports the retained diagnosis of MAN. Stained with Hematoxylin and Eosin (H&E) at 200x magnification.
cleaned: Epidermal acanthosis and hyperpigmentation of basal cells, particularly at rete pegs. Hyperpigmentation of the basement membrane with numerous melanophages. No cytonuclear atypia or melanocytic hyperplasia. MAN.

label: melanoma
original: Histopathological image showing hematoxylin and eosin (H&E) staining of tumor cells from Case 1, magnified 400x. These tumor cells tested positive for estrogen receptor (ER) and progesterone receptor (PR), with expression observed in their nuclei. This melanoma developed and spread rapidly during pregnancy.
cleaned:
Spindle-shaped and epithelioid tumor cells arranged in nests and fascicles. Cells exhibit prominent nucleoli and moderate amounts of cytoplasm. Pigment present. 

Melanoma.

label: melanoma
original: Histopathological image showing hematoxylin and eosin (H&E) staining of tumor cells from Case 2, magnified 400x. These tumor cells tested positive for estrogen receptor (ER) and progesterone receptor (PR), with expression observed in their nuclei. This melanoma developed and spread rapidly during pregnancy.
cleaned:
Spindle-shaped and epithelioid tumor cells with hyperchromatic nuclei. Cells are arranged in nests and fascicles. Pigment present. 

Melanoma.

label: melanoma
original: Histopathological image showing hematoxylin and eosin (H&E) staining of tumor cells from Case 3, magnified 400x. These tumor cells tested positive for estrogen receptor (ER) and progesterone receptor (PR), with expression observed in their nuclei. This melanoma developed and spread rapidly after childbirth.
cleaned:
Spindle-shaped and epithelioid cells with hyperchromatic nuclei arranged in nests and fascicles. Pigment present. Tumor cells infiltrating the surrounding stroma. 
Melanoma.

label: melanoma
original: Thyroid histology showing melanoma metastasis. This image depicts one of the multiple bilateral cancer foci found in the thyroid, confirming a diagnosis of multinodular goiter. The metastatic melanoma displays characteristics overlapping with the primary nasal mucosa neoplasm and showed immunoreactivity for HMB-45. (H&E staining, x200 magnification).
cleaned: Thyroid tissue with dark brown to black neoplastic cells arranged in irregular clusters. Cells exhibit pleomorphism and prominent nucleoli. Diagnosis: melanoma metastasis, multinodular goiter.

label: melanoma
original: Thyroid histology showing neoplastic cells, identified as melanoma metastasis, infiltrating the parenchyma without destroying the thyroid follicles. These epithelioid or spindle cells exhibit granular chromatin and scant cytoplasm. This image represents one of the multiple bilateral cancer foci found in the thyroid, confirming a diagnosis of multinodular goiter. The tumor's characteristics overlap with the primary nasal mucosa neoplasm and it showed immunoreactivity for HMB-45. (H&E staining, x400 magnification).
cleaned: Neoplastic cells infiltrating the parenchyma, without destroying thyroid follicles. Epithelioid or spindle cells with granular chromatin and scant cytoplasm. Multinodular goiter. Melanoma metastasis.

label: melanoma
original: Histopathological image showing melanoma infiltrating prostate glands. The tumor cells present with round nuclei, prominent nucleoli, and moderate cytoplasm. Both intracellular and extracellular deposits of melanin pigment are visible. [H&E, 400X]
cleaned: Tumor cells infiltrating prostate glands. Cells display round nuclei, prominent nucleoli, and moderate cytoplasm. Intracellular and extracellular melanin pigment is visible. Melanoma.

label: nevus
original: H&E-stained section (100x) showing histopathological features suggestive of intramucosal nevi. The image displays the proliferation of nevus cells, characterized by cohesive islands of polygonal to oval epithelioid cells with focal melanin pigment production. Nests of nevus cells are separated by a hyalinised band beneath the stratified squamous surface epithelium. The nevus cells exhibit junctional separation and differentiation, without significant cellular atypia or dysplasia. The associated fibrous connective tissue contains dense bundles of collagen fibres. A yellow arrow points to Type A cells, which are large and round, without dendritic processes, located at the periphery adjacent to the epithelium with intense melanin accumulation. A blue arrow points to Type B cells, which are uniform, round to polygonal, found in deeper layers, and show decreased melanin deposition. Lesional cells are observed close to the excision borders.
cleaned: Proliferation of nevus cells in cohesive islands of polygonal to oval epithelioid cells with focal melanin pigment. Nests of nevus cells are separated by a hyalinised band beneath stratified squamous surface epithelium. Nevus cells exhibit junctional separation and differentiation. Dense collagen fibres are present in fibrous connective tissue. Large, round cells with intense melanin accumulation are located peripherally adjacent to the epithelium. Uniform, round to polygonal cells with decreased melanin deposition are found in deeper layers. Lesional cells are observed close to excision borders. Intramucosal nevi.

label: melanoma
original: Histopathological examination of excised tissue revealing atypical fusiform and epithelioid melanocytes containing granular or dense melanin pigment. These melanocytes extended from the lower dermis to the subcutaneous layer. These findings are consistent with a malignant melanoma arising in association with PTBN. The image is stained with hematoxylin and eosin, original magnification ×40.
cleaned: Fusiform and epithelioid melanocytes with granular and dense melanin pigment extending from the lower dermis to the subcutaneous layer. Malignant melanoma.

label: melanoma
original: Histopathologic aspects of a nail matrix biopsy showing nail melanoma. The image displays proliferation of atypical melanocytes in the basal and supra-basal layers of the nail matrix. Nail melanoma often presents clinically as longitudinal melanonychia, a brown to black band of the nail plate. Histologically, both melanin granules and atypical (pagetoid) melanocytes can be observed in nail melanoma. Stained with Hematoxylin and Eosin (H&E), original magnification 10X.
cleaned:
Proliferation of atypical melanocytes in the basal and supra-basal layers of the nail matrix. Brown to black melanin granules and atypical (pagetoid) melanocytes are visible. 
Diagnosis: Nail melanoma.

label: melanoma
original: Histopathologic aspects of a nail matrix biopsy showing nail melanoma. The image displays proliferation of atypical melanocytes in the basal and supra-basal layers of the nail matrix. Nail melanoma often presents clinically as longitudinal melanonychia, a brown to black band of the nail plate. Histologically, both melanin granules and atypical (pagetoid) melanocytes can be observed in nail melanoma. Stained with Hematoxylin and Eosin (H&E), original magnification 25X.
cleaned:
Proliferation of atypical melanocytes in the basal and supra-basal layers of the nail matrix. Brown to black melanin granules and atypical (pagetoid) melanocytes are observed. 
Diagnosis: Nail melanoma.

label: melanoma
original: Histopathological examination (Hematoxylin and Eosin stain, original magnification 5x) of the ulcerated plaque from the left leg of a 58-year-old woman, revealing irregular hyperplastic epidermis. In the dermis, large nests of BerEp4-positive basaloid cells are present, alongside a second cell population consisting of spindle cells and dendritic cells. These spindle and dendritic cells stained positive for Melan-A, S100, and HMB-45, representing a melanoma component. A heavy inflammatory infiltrate is also observed in the perilesional area.
cleaned: Irregular hyperplastic epidermis. Large nests of basaloid cells in the dermis, alongside spindle cells and dendritic cells. Melanoma component present. Heavy inflammatory infiltrate.

label: melanoma
original: Higher magnification (Hematoxylin and Eosin stain, original magnification 20x) of the histopathological examination of the ulcerated plaque from the left leg of a 58-year-old woman. This image shows the morphology of BerEp4-positive basaloid cells, as well as atypical spindle and dendritic cells (which stained positive for Melan-A, S100, and HMB-45) with visible mitotic figures, indicating proliferation within both the basal cell carcinoma and melanoma components.
cleaned: Basaloid cells with atypical spindle and dendritic cells. Mitotic figures are visible. Both basal cell carcinoma and melanoma components are present.

label: nevus
original: Histologic image showing a lentiginous intraepidermal proliferation of melanocytes on the right (blue arrowheads) and a benign intradermal nevus on the left (yellow star). This image illustrates a case with only slight atypia upon final histologic evaluation. The image is stained with Hematoxylin and Eosin (H&E) at an original magnification of 40x.
cleaned: Lentiginous intraepidermal proliferation of melanocytes and a benign intradermal nevus.

label: melanoma
original: Histopathology of an in situ melanoma on the left leg, stained with Hematoxylin & eosin (×100 magnification). The image shows intense cytological atypia and exuberant pagetoid dissemination, which is characteristic of in situ melanoma, and importantly, observed without dermal invasion.
cleaned: Atypical melanocytes with pagetoid dissemination are present within the epidermis, without dermal invasion. Diagnosis: in situ melanoma.

label: melanoma
original: Histopathology of Nodular Melanoma (NM) showing ulceration of the epidermis overlying the tumor, characterized by fibrin deposition and neutrophils. Tumor ulceration was a significant finding, present in 47 (59.5%) of the melanomas studied. (Hematoxylin and Eosin stain, original magnification × 100).
cleaned: Epidermis with ulceration and fibrin deposition, neutrophils present. Underlying tumor composed of atypical melanocytes. Diagnosis: Nodular Melanoma.

label: melanoma
original: Histopathology of Nodular Melanoma (NM) demonstrating a high mitotic rate. High mitotic rates, specifically ≥5 mitoses/mm², were observed in 38 cases (48.1%) of the melanomas and were frequently associated with lymph node metastases (22/34 cases with metastases had this rate). (Hematoxylin and Eosin stain, original magnification × 400).
cleaned: Nodular Melanoma demonstrating a high mitotic rate. Cells exhibit pleomorphic nuclei and abundant cytoplasm. Tissue architecture shows a dense proliferation of atypical melanocytes.

label: melanoma
original: Histopathology of Superficial Spreading Melanoma (SSM) showing brisk tumor-infiltrating lymphocytes (TILs). Brisk TILs were present in 26 cases (32.9%) of the melanomas studied. (Hematoxylin and Eosin stain, original magnification × 100).
cleaned: Tumor-infiltrating lymphocytes are present. Melanoma.

label: melanoma
original: Histopathology demonstrating lymph nodal metastasis of melanoma, characterized by the presence of melanic pigment deposition. Lymph node metastases were observed in 34 patients (43.1%) and were frequently associated with ulcerated primary melanomas and high mitotic rates. Loco-regional lymph node status also correlated positively with tumor thickness (pT) and Clark level. (Hematoxylin and Eosin stain, original magnification × 200).
cleaned: Lymph node with dark brown pigment deposition. Tumor cells present within the lymph node architecture. Diagnosis: melanoma metastasis.

label: melanoma
original: Histopathology of a liver section from patient 1, showing micrometastases of uveal melanoma. The micrometastases predominantly display an infiltrative or replacement growth pattern. The framed area within this image is shown at higher magnification in Panel B. This image provides an example of micrometastases of uveal melanoma, a condition detected in 10 out of 11 (91%) patients in the study. Stained with hematoxylin and eosin (H&E). Magnification, ×5.
cleaned: Infiltrative and replacement growth pattern of dark brown pigmented cells within liver tissue architecture. Micrometastases of uveal melanoma.

label: melanoma
original: Histopathology of a liver section from patient 1, showing micrometastases of uveal melanoma. This panel shows a higher magnification (×40) view of the framed area indicated in Panel A, illustrating the predominantly infiltrative or replacement growth pattern of the micrometastases. This image provides an example of micrometastases of uveal melanoma, a condition detected in 10 out of 11 (91%) patients in the study. Stained with hematoxylin and eosin (H&E). Magnification, ×40.
cleaned: Infiltrative or replacement growth pattern of micrometastases. Micrometastases of uveal melanoma.

label: melanoma
original: Histopathology of a liver section from patient 1, showing micrometastases of uveal melanoma. A nodular growth pattern (previously described as desmoplastic and pushing growth pattern), which was less common, is displayed here. The framed area within this image is shown at higher magnification in Panel D. This image provides an example of micrometastases of uveal melanoma, a condition detected in 10 out of 11 (91%) patients in the study. Stained with hematoxylin and eosin (H&E). Magnification, ×5.
cleaned: Nodular growth pattern. Micrometastases of uveal melanoma.

label: melanoma
original: Histopathology of a liver section from patient 1, showing micrometastases of uveal melanoma. This panel shows a higher magnification (×40) view of the framed area indicated in Panel C, illustrating the less common nodular growth pattern (previously described as desmoplastic and pushing growth pattern) of the micrometastases. This image provides an example of micrometastases of uveal melanoma, a condition detected in 10 out of 11 (91%) patients in the study. Stained with hematoxylin and eosin (H&E). Magnification, ×40.
cleaned: Nodular growth pattern of micrometastases. Dark brown cells within liver tissue architecture. Uveal melanoma.

label: melanoma
original: Histopathology of a myocardium section from patient 3, showing micrometastases of uveal melanoma. The tumor cells appear in sheets with an infiltrative pattern. The framed area within this image is shown at higher magnification in Panel F. This image provides an example of micrometastases of uveal melanoma, a condition detected in 10 out of 11 (91%) patients in the study. Stained with hematoxylin and eosin (H&E). Magnification, ×5.
cleaned: Sheets of tumor cells with an infiltrative pattern. Uveal melanoma.

label: melanoma
original: Histopathology of a myocardium section from patient 3, showing micrometastases of uveal melanoma. This panel shows a higher magnification (×40) view of the framed area indicated in Panel E, illustrating sheets of infiltrative tumor cells. This image provides an example of micrometastases of uveal melanoma, a condition detected in 10 out of 11 (91%) patients in the study. Stained with hematoxylin and eosin (H&E). Magnification, ×40.
cleaned: Sheets of infiltrative tumor cells. Uveal melanoma.

label: melanoma
original: Histopathology image showing irregular proliferation of atypical melanocytes within the epidermis, some of them arranged in nests (haematoxylin and eosin, original magnification x20). An atypical mitosis is observed in the inset. This image corresponds to a lesion from a 51-year-old woman that was surgically removed sixteen months into a surveillance program, and histopathologically diagnosed as a melanoma with a Breslow thickness of 0.18mm (pT1a).
cleaned:
Irregular proliferation of atypical melanocytes within the epidermis, some arranged in nests. Atypical mitosis is observed.

Melanoma.

label: melanoma
original: Histopathology image showing the extension of atypical melanocytes down adnexal structures (H&E, original magnification x20). This image corresponds to a lesion from a 51-year-old woman that was surgically removed sixteen months into a surveillance program, and histopathologically diagnosed as a melanoma with a Breslow thickness of 0.18mm (pT1a).
cleaned: Atypical melanocytes extending down adnexal structures. Melanoma.

label: melanoma
original: Histopathology showing desmoplastic melanoma, stained with Hematoxylin and Eosin (H&E), viewed at x100 magnification.
cleaned: Spindle and epithelioid cells arranged in fascicles within a desmoplastic stroma. Tumor cells exhibit prominent nucleoli and hyperchromatic nuclei. Diagnosis: desmoplastic melanoma.

label: melanoma
original: Histopathology showing desmoplastic melanoma, stained with Hematoxylin and Eosin (H&E), viewed at x400 magnification.
cleaned: Spindle and epithelioid cells arranged in fascicles and nests within a desmoplastic stroma. Tumor cells exhibit prominent nucleoli and hyperchromatic nuclei. Diagnosis: desmoplastic melanoma.

label: melanoma
original: Histopathology showing superficial spreading melanoma at x100 magnification, stained with Hematoxylin and Eosin (H&E).
cleaned:
Asymmetric proliferation of atypical melanocytes with large nuclei and prominent nucleoli extending into the papillary dermis. Tumor cells exhibit variable pigmentation and irregular nest patterns. 

Diagnosis: Melanoma.

label: melanoma
original: Histopathology showing acral lentiginous melanoma, stained with hematoxylin and eosin (H&E) at x100 magnification.
cleaned: Melanoma with atypical melanocytes exhibiting pleomorphism and hyperchromatic nuclei. Lesion displays irregular borders and nests of melanocytes within the epidermis and dermis.

label: melanoma
original: Histopathology showing lentigo maligna melanoma (LMM) with hematoxylin and eosin (H&E) staining, viewed at x100 magnification.
cleaned: Lentigo maligna melanoma with atypical melanocytes exhibiting large nuclei and prominent nucleoli, arranged in nests and single cells infiltrating the epidermis and superficial dermis.

label: melanoma
original: Histopathological examination of a hyperpigmented lesion showing an H&E-stained section at 100x magnification. The image reveals an atypical melanocytic lesion composed of aggregates of pigmented cells (denoted by arrows) in the superficial lamina propria, located between or around benign pits. These aggregates consist of atypical melanocytes exhibiting nuclear atypia. The overall findings from such lesions were confirmatory of malignant melanoma, which also harbored a BRAF V60E mutation identified by targeted next-generation sequencing.
cleaned: Aggregates of pigmented cells in the superficial lamina propria, located between or around benign pits. Atypical melanocytes exhibiting nuclear atypia. Malignant melanoma.

label: melanoma
original: Histopathology of malignant melanoma metastasis showing melanoma cells forming nests of varying sizes. Some nests display a pseudoglandular pattern (arrows), a feature observed in only one of the five melanoma cases studied. The neoplastic cells in this amelanotic melanoma appear without prominent melanin pigment. Nuclear pleomorphism was detected in all cases. Original magnification, x100; Hematoxylin and Eosin (H&E) stain.
cleaned: Melanoma cells forming nests of varying sizes. Some nests display a pseudoglandular pattern. Neoplastic cells lack prominent melanin pigment. Nuclear pleomorphism is present. Diagnosis: melanoma.

label: melanoma
original: Histopathology of malignant melanoma metastasis, depicting neoplastic melanoma cells infiltrating the lamina propria. The neoplastic cells are small and show minimal nuclear pleomorphism, consistent with nuclear pleomorphism being detected in all five cases. This represents a diffuse/interstitial growth pattern, observed in four of the five studied melanoma cases, which also appeared amelanotic. Original magnification, x100; Hematoxylin and Eosin (H&E) stain.
cleaned: Neoplastic melanoma cells infiltrating the lamina propria. Cells are small with minimal nuclear pleomorphism. Diffuse/interstitial growth pattern. Amelanotic. Malignant melanoma metastasis.

label: melanoma
original: Histopathology of malignant melanoma metastasis, illustrating melanoma cells with prominent nuclear pleomorphism and intranuclear inclusions (arrows). Intranuclear pseudoinclusions were observed in two of the five cases studied, and nuclear pleomorphism was detected in all cases. The neoplastic cells in this amelanotic melanoma appear without prominent melanin pigment. Original magnification, x400; Hematoxylin and Eosin (H&E) stain.
cleaned: Melanoma cells with prominent nuclear pleomorphism and intranuclear inclusions. Neoplastic cells lack prominent melanin pigment.

label: melanoma
original: Histopathology of superficial spreading melanoma. Nests of atypical tumor cells are observed at the dermo-epidermal junction and extending into the papillary dermis. The tumor cells are typically organized in nests and exhibit marked atypia, various sizes and shapes, eosinophilic cytoplasm, and nuclear pleomorphism, with characteristic features such as prominent eosinophilic nucleoli. Hematoxylin and eosin (H&E) staining, original magnification ×100.
cleaned:
Nests of atypical tumor cells at the dermo-epidermal junction and extending into the papillary dermis. Tumor cells are organized in nests and exhibit atypia, various sizes and shapes, eosinophilic cytoplasm, and nuclear pleomorphism with prominent eosinophilic nucleoli. 

Superficial spreading melanoma.

label: melanoma
original: Histopathology of nodular melanoma, a tumor type with the worst prognosis, showing nests of tumoral cells with abundant eosinophilic cytoplasm. The cells are typically medium or large in size, with enlarged nuclei that are sometimes irregular in shape and hyperchromic, displaying severe nuclear pleomorphism. A distinctive feature, eosinophilic nucleoli, are prominent. Stained with Hematoxylin–Eosin (HE) at ×200 magnification.
cleaned: Nests of tumoral cells with abundant eosinophilic cytoplasm. Cells are medium to large in size, with enlarged, sometimes irregular, hyperchromic nuclei displaying severe nuclear pleomorphism. Prominent eosinophilic nucleoli are visible. Diagnosis: melanoma.

label: melanoma
original: Histopathology of nodular melanoma, a tumor type with the worst prognosis. The image shows nests of tumoral cells, part of a solid tumor proliferation, occupying the dermis. The delimitation between the tumor and the epidermis can be clearly seen. The tumoral cells are typically medium or large in size, often presenting with pale or eosinophilic cytoplasm and enlarged nuclei that can be irregular in shape and hyperchromic, frequently displaying eosinophilic nucleoli. Stained with Hematoxylin–Eosin (HE) at ×100 magnification.
cleaned:
Nests of tumoral cells occupying the dermis, with a clear delimitation between the tumor and the epidermis. Tumoral cells are medium to large in size with pale or eosinophilic cytoplasm and enlarged, irregular, hyperchromic nuclei, frequently displaying eosinophilic nucleoli.
Diagnosis: melanoma.

label: melanoma
original: Histopathology of Paget disease (hematoxylin and eosin stain). The image shows nests of pale-staining tumor cells, also known as Paget’s cells, arranged singly or in small clusters along the vulvar epidermis. These tumor cells typically have abundant pale cytoplasm and large nuclei with a prominent, vesicular nucleus, and may display unusual mitotic figures. The tumor commonly locates in the epidermis, with occasional dermal invasion. This histopathological diagnosis is crucial as this entity can be confused with other neoplasms such as melanoma, Bowen’s disease, or sebaceous carcinoma.
cleaned: Nests of pale-staining tumor cells arranged singly or in small clusters along the epidermis. Tumor cells have abundant pale cytoplasm and large nuclei with prominent, vesicular nuclei, and may display unusual mitotic figures. Occasional dermal invasion is present. Diagnosis: Paget disease.

label: nevus
original: Histopathology (Hematoxylin Eosin, original magnification 70x) showing Juvenile Localized Scleroderma (JLS). This panel illustrates compact fibrosis involving dermal and subcutaneous layers (long arrow) and the disappearance of skin adnexa. These features are characteristic of JLS and were key in differentiating it from Connective Tissue Nevus (CTN) during histological review of patient biopsies, some of which were initially misdiagnosed.
cleaned: Compact fibrosis involving dermal and subcutaneous layers and disappearance of skin adnexa. Juvenile Localized Scleroderma.

label: nevus
original: Histopathology (Hematoxylin Eosin, original magnification 200x) showing Juvenile Localized Scleroderma (JLS). This panel reveals perivascular inflammatory infiltrates (short arrow). The presence of significant perivascular inflammatory infiltrate is a crucial feature distinguishing JLS from Connective Tissue Nevus (CTN), where such infiltrates are typically absent, and was vital for correct diagnosis in patients initially misdiagnosed with JLS.
cleaned: Perivascular inflammatory infiltrates. Juvenile Localized Scleroderma.

label: nevus
original: Histopathology (Hematoxylin Eosin, original magnification 70x) showing Non-familial collagenoma, a type of Connective Tissue Nevus (CTN). This panel demonstrates thickened collagen bundles arranged randomly in the reticular dermis (long arrow). The presence of grossly thickened collagen fibers is a most important histological feature that allowed confirmation of CTN diagnosis in all patients, many of whom were initially suspected of having Juvenile Localized Scleroderma (JLS).
cleaned: Thickened collagen bundles arranged randomly in the reticular dermis. Collagenoma. Connective Tissue Nevus.

label: nevus
original: Histopathology (Hematoxylin Eosin, original magnification 200x) showing Non-familial collagenoma, a type of Connective Tissue Nevus (CTN). This panel highlights preserved skin adnexa and the absence of inflammatory infiltrate (short arrow). The normal representation of skin adnexa and the absence of significant perivascular and perineural inflammatory infiltrate are crucial features that helped differentiate CTN from Juvenile Localized Scleroderma (JLS) and ultimately led to the correct diagnosis in patients undergoing deep skin biopsies and histological review.
cleaned: Preserved skin adnexa. Absence of inflammatory infiltrate. Collagenoma. Connective Tissue Nevus.

label: melanoma
original: Histopathology of the penile specimen (H&E stain, 70x magnification) from a 62-year-old male patient diagnosed with mucosal melanoma of the glans penis. This low-magnification image demonstrates a highly cellular anaplastic tumor originating from the surface of the glans and invading the surrounding structures. Histopathological examination showed severe cytological atypia and numerous mitotic figures. The gross specimen was a 29 mm slightly pigmented and partially ulcerated white-brown heterogenous mass in the distal urethra.
cleaned: Highly cellular anaplastic tumor invading surrounding structures originating from the surface of the glans penis. Severe cytological atypia and numerous mitotic figures are present. White-brown heterogenous mass with areas of ulceration. Mucosal melanoma.

label: melanoma
original: Histopathology of the penile specimen (H&E stain, 200x magnification) from a 62-year-old male patient diagnosed with mucosal melanoma of the glans penis. The anaplastic tumor is composed of epithelioid and spindle cells, with areas displaying either dominant epithelioid or spindle cell morphology. Brown intracytoplasmic pigment is present, and bizarre tumor cells can be occasionally observed. The tumor exhibited varying amounts of extra- and intracellular pigment. The inset (SOX-10 immunohistochemistry, 200x magnification) reveals diffuse positivity of tumor cells with SOX-10, confirming the melanocytic origin.
cleaned: Anaplastic tumor composed of epithelioid and spindle cells, with areas displaying either dominant epithelioid or spindle cell morphology. Brown intracytoplasmic pigment is present, and bizarre tumor cells are occasionally observed. Varying amounts of extra- and intracellular pigment are visible. Mucosal melanoma.

label: melanoma
original: Histopathology of the penile specimen (H&E stain, 400x magnification) from a 62-year-old male patient diagnosed with mucosal melanoma of the glans penis. Brisk mitotic activity with atypical cell divisions is evident, indicated by the yellow arrowhead. Histopathological examination revealed severe cytological atypia and numerous mitotic figures. The inset presents Ki-67-positive cells (Ki-67 immunohistochemistry, 200x magnification) with a proliferation index estimated at 80% positivity in the hot-spot areas, indicating rapid tumor growth.
cleaned: Atypical cells with brisk mitotic activity and numerous mitotic figures. Severe cytological atypia is present. Areas of positive cells are visible. Mucosal melanoma.

label: melanoma
original: Histopathology of the penile specimen (H&E stain, 200x magnification) from a 62-year-old male patient diagnosed with mucosal melanoma of the glans penis. Below the non-keratinizing squamous epithelium of the glans, an in situ component and pagetoid spread are observed, specifically found in the epithelium of the external urethral orifice. Lymphovascular invasion was also noted at the tumor’s edge. The tumor cells display diffuse PRAME expression (PRAME immunohistochemistry, 100x magnification), which is another marker for melanoma.
cleaned: Non-keratinizing squamous epithelium with underlying in situ component and pagetoid spread within the epithelium of the external urethral orifice. Tumor cells display diffuse expression. Mucosal melanoma.

label: melanoma
original: Histopathology of the foreskin biopsy from a 68-year-old male patient, confirming mucosal melanoma. Below the mucosa, an atypical melanocytic proliferation is visible. The melanocytes form irregular nests of various sizes and are present at multiple levels of the mucosa [black arrowheads], consistent with pagetoid spread (H&E stain, 100x).
cleaned: Atypical melanocytic proliferation below the mucosa forming irregular nests of various sizes at multiple levels. Pagetoid spread is present. Mucosal melanoma.

label: melanoma
original: Histopathology of the foreskin biopsy from a 68-year-old male patient, confirming mucosal melanoma. The cytological atypia is severe, with noticeable epidermal thinning. Tumor cells extend to the deeper layers of the foreskin. The Breslow thickness was measured as 1.0 mm, with one mitosis per square millimeter, classifying the tumor stage as pT1b. A moderate lymphocytic infiltrate surrounds the lesion (H&E stain, 200x).
cleaned:
Epidermal thinning with severe cytological atypia. Tumor cells extend into deeper layers. Moderate lymphocytic infiltrate surrounds the lesion. 
Diagnosis: Melanoma.

label: melanoma
original: Histopathology showing a malignant melanotic nerve sheath tumor (MMNST). The image displays tumor cells with epithelioid morphology and melanin granules within their cytoplasm, as seen with H&E staining. MMNST presents a diagnostic challenge due to its histologic overlap with other conditions such as metastatic melanoma, primary leptomeningeal melanocytic neoplasms, melanocytoma, and conventional Schwannoma. An accurate diagnosis is crucial for appropriate clinical course, management, prognosis, and potential genetic counseling.
cleaned: Tumor cells with epithelioid morphology and melanin granules within their cytoplasm. Malignant melanotic nerve sheath tumor (MMNST).

label: melanoma
original: Histological examination of penile tissue, stained with Haematoxylin and eosin, at original magnification x20, showing features in keeping with lichen sclerosus in a 63-year-old man with superficial spreading melanoma. This panel specifically illustrates thinning and attenuation of the surface squamous epithelium.
cleaned: Thinning and attenuation of the surface squamous epithelium. Superficial spreading melanoma. Lichen sclerosus.

label: melanoma
original: Histological examination of penile tissue, stained with Haematoxylin and eosin, at original magnification x20, showing features in keeping with lichen sclerosus in a 63-year-old man with superficial spreading melanoma. This panel specifically illustrates subepithelial sclerosis.
cleaned: Subepithelial sclerosis. Lichen sclerosus. Melanoma.

label: melanoma
original: Histological examination of penile tissue, stained with Haematoxylin and eosin, at original magnification x20, showing features in keeping with lichen sclerosus in a 63-year-old man with superficial spreading melanoma. This panel specifically illustrates a lichenoid chronic inflammatory cell infiltrate.
cleaned: Lichenoid chronic inflammatory cell infiltrate. Superficial spreading melanoma.

label: melanoma
original: Histological examination of a biopsy specimen taken from the polypoid nodule on the left cheek of an 81-year-old Japanese woman. The image shows a proliferation of atypical spindle cells and infiltrating lymphocytes on the dermis, features that contributed to the diagnosis of malignant melanoma (MM) at stage IIC. The section is stained with hematoxylin-and-eosin; original magnification, ×100.

label: melanoma
original: Histopathology showing cerebroid nests of melanocytes (CNMs) in nodular melanoma, observed during a morphological comparison between poorly differentiated clusters (PDCs) and CNMs. CNMs are readily detectable and display a morphology similar to PDCs in colorectal carcinoma. These CNMs, an active growth front of nodular melanoma, are characterized by atypical pleomorphic melanocytic cells with large cytoplasm, spindle or epithelioid shape, and prominent nucleoli, often detaching from the main mass with an infiltrative appearance. CNMs are associated with lymphatic vascular invasion, higher Clark level (Clark’s IV and V), epithelioid phenotype, aggressive growth, and clinically characterized by recurrence and metastasis. The image is a Hematoxylin and eosin stained slide at 20x magnification, with a scale bar of 100 µm.

label: nevus
original: Histopathology of an intramucosal nevus stained with Hematoxylin & eosin (scale bar: 50 μm). The image shows nests of pigmented nevus cells that are clearly separated from the epithelial lining by an evident basement membrane. The subepithelial region contains polygonal and epithelioid cells, while deeper cells resemble lymphocytes. Oral melanocytic nevi are rare benign neoplasms, often found on the palate, and are diagnosed on average in the third and fourth decades of life. Histologically, intramucosal nevi frequently exhibit cells arranged in nests, with polygonal and epithelioid cell types observed. Small, dense nuclei resembling lymphocytes (Type B nevus cells) are also characteristic, and melanin pigment is commonly present within the cytoplasm.

label: nevus
original: Histopathology of an intramucosal nevus stained with Hematoxylin & eosin (scale bar: 50 μm), revealing deep-seated angled, spindle-shaped cells with neurotization, characteristic of Type C nevus cells. Oral melanocytic nevi are rare benign neoplasms, and the morphology of nevus cells varies depending on their location and stage of maturation. While intramucosal nevi commonly show polygonal and epithelioid cells, the presence of spindle-shaped cells with neurotization (Type C cells) is also a recognized feature, observed in some cases in this study and associated with the maturation process. Neurotization was found in two cases of intramucosal nevus, and such lesions typically exhibit symmetry, an important feature for defining them as benign.
cleaned: Deep-seated, angled, spindle-shaped cells with neurotization. Intramucosal nevus. Type C nevus cells.

Blue nevus was the most frequent type of nevus identified in this study (46.67%, n=7), showing a female predilection (80%) and commonly located on the palate. These lesions are typically dark or black. Histologically, blue nevi are characterized by spindle-shaped cells often arranged parallel to the surface, and the presence of dendritic melanocytes is not uncommon in the oral mucosa, consistent with the brownish melanic pigment observed.

label: nevus
original: Histopathology by H&E stain showing involvement of the dermis predominantly by spindle-shaped Schwannian cells and small nests of round melanocytes at 40X magnification. The histopathological examination revealed extensive involvement of the dermis by small nests of spindle-shaped melanocytes. Increased vascularity was noted, along with clusters of small, round melanocytes interspersed between collagen bundles and around epithelial adnexal structures, extending into the subcutaneous septa and between adipocytes. Rare pagetoid arrays of melanocytes were also observed as both single cells and round nests, with pale, vacuolated cytoplasm and dusty melanin. No alarming features, such as increased mitotic activity or perineural invasion, were identified. These findings were interpreted as NCH (Nodular Cellular Nevus) with Schwannian differentiation and focal mild atypical features within a CMN (Congenital Melanocytic Nevus), highlighting the complexity of this lesion.

original: Histopathological image of tumoral melanosis (TM) at ×10 magnification, stained with hematoxylin and eosin. The image is characterized by mild stromal reorganization with fibrosis, lymphoid inflammation with rare plasma cells, and massive accumulation of melanophages. From a histological point of view, TM is considered a complete regression of melanoma, characterized by the complete absence of dermal or junctional melanocytes, with the lesion area entirely replaced by varying degrees of fibrosis, inflammation, and sometimes abundant melanophages in the site of a previously documented pigmented lesion.
cleaned: Mild stromal reorganization with fibrosis, lymphoid inflammation with rare plasma cells, and massive accumulation of melanophages. Fibrosis, inflammation, and abundant melanophages replace the lesion area. Tumoral melanosis.

label: melanoma

label: melanoma

label: melanoma
original: Histological image (hematoxylin-eosin, 10× magnification) of an NRAS- and CCND1 gene-mutated melanoma, 4.9 mm-thick with a mitotic rate of 11/mm², from the forearm of an 80-year-old woman. The image displays dilated capillaries (arrows) located beneath the epidermis and extending between tumor dermal aggregates. These dilated capillaries are a histological manifestation of the vascular patterns seen dermoscopically and are significantly associated with mutations in cell cycle genes like CCND1. This neoangiogenesis reflects the tumor's adaptive response to the increasing oxygen and nutrient demands of rapidly proliferating melanoma cells, often under hypoxic conditions, leading to the activation of factors like HIF-1α that induce the expression of genes involved in angiogenesis and melanoma progression.
cleaned: Tumor dermal aggregates beneath the epidermis with dilated capillaries. Tumor cells exhibit variable morphology. Diagnosis: melanoma.

Atypical melanocytes were also observed in the reticular dermis (Clark level 4). These histological findings contributed to the suspicion of invasive melanoma (pT3b).
cleaned: Melanocytes infiltrating and disrupting the epidermis. Nests of atypical melanocytes are present in the epidermis and dermis, with ulceration and pagetoid spread of melanocytes within the epidermis. Atypical melanocytes are observed in the reticular dermis. Invasive melanoma.

label: melanoma
original: Histopathology of nodular melanoma, stained with hematoxylin-eosin at 10× magnification. The image displays a densely packed dermal proliferation of atypical melanocytes extending to the reticular dermis, consistent with Clark’s level IV and a Breslow thickness of 2.9 mm. Several dermal mitoses are evident, counted at 10 mitoses per 1 mm². Nodular melanoma lesions frequently exhibit such dermal mitosis and may also present with epidermal ulceration. Histologically, a key diagnostic feature for nodular melanoma is that if an intraepidermal component is present, it should not extend for more than three rete ridges beyond the dermal component.
cleaned: Densely packed dermal proliferation of atypical melanocytes extending to the reticular dermis. Several dermal mitoses are evident. Nodular melanoma.

label: melanoma

The melanoma exhibits moderate non-brisk peritumoral and intratumoral inflammatory infiltrate. Histologically, superficial spreading melanoma is characterized by asymmetric, poorly demarcated melanocytes that lack cellular maturation. Melanocytes are distributed unevenly, often with solitary melanocytes being more predominant over nests, and commonly with evidence of intraepithelial spread in a pagetoid manner.
cleaned: Asymmetric, poorly demarcated melanocytes lacking cellular maturation infiltrating the superficial dermis. Melanocytes are distributed unevenly, with solitary melanocytes more predominant over nests, and evidence of intraepithelial spread. Superficial spreading melanoma.

label: melanoma

label: melanoma

label: melanoma
original: Histopathology of metastatic malignant melanoma (MM) showing spindle-shaped cells interwoven with epithelioid cells. Stained with hematoxylin and eosin (HE) at ×400 magnification.

original: Histopathology of the axillary metastatic malignant melanoma (MM) mass, predominantly composed of spindle-shaped cells. Stained with hematoxylin and eosin (HE) at ×200 magnification.

label: melanoma

Systematic pathology protocols and clinical vigilance are highlighted as vital for early detection of this rare and diagnostically elusive malignancy, even when prognosis remains poor.

label: nevus

label: melanoma
original: Histopathology of a resected gastric polyp from a 75-year-old man, diagnosed as malignant melanoma, stained with hematoxylin and eosin (HE) at 20×10 magnification, revealing melanoma cells with irregular morphology, melanin granules within the cytoplasm, and nuclear pleomorphism and atypia.

original: Histopathology of a resected gastric polyp from a 75-year-old man, diagnosed as malignant melanoma, stained with hematoxylin and eosin (HE) at 20×10 magnification, revealing melanoma cells with irregular morphology, melanin granules within the cytoplasm, and nuclear pleomorphism and atypia.

label: nevus

label: melanoma
original: Microscopic image of histopathology for Case 21, an 82-year-old male presenting with lentigo maligna melanoma (LMM) on the forehead. The image, stained with H&E and viewed at 100x magnification, shows the tumor at Level 1 with a Breslow thickness of 0.5 mm.
cleaned: Tumor at Level 1 with a Breslow thickness of 0.5 mm. Lentigo maligna melanoma.

label: melanoma
original: Microscopic image of histopathology for Case 21, an 82-year-old male presenting with lentigo maligna melanoma (LMM) on the forehead. Atypical melanocytes with large nuclei and prominent nucleoli are present within the epidermis and extending into the dermis. The lesion exhibits irregular nest patterns and effacement of the dermal-epidermal junction. Tumor cells display hyperpigmentation and variation in cell size and shape. 

Diagnosis: Lentigo maligna melanoma.

label: melanoma
original: Microscopic image of histopathology for Case 39, an 82-year-old male with lentigo maligna melanoma (LMM) on the arm. The overall Breslow thickness for this case was 1.5 mm.
cleaned: Incipient ulceration with fibrin and epidermal consumption. Melanoma.

label: melanoma
original: Microscopic image of histopathology for Case 39, an 82-year-old male with lentigo maligna melanoma (LMM) on the arm. The presence of ulceration (also seen in incipient form in panel c) resulted in the upstaging of the patient from pT2a to pT2b. The overall Breslow thickness for this case was 1.5 mm.
cleaned: Ulceration of epidermis with atypical melanocytes. Lesion extends into the papillary dermis. Lentigo maligna melanoma.

label: melanoma
original: Microscopic image of histopathology for Case 31, a 79-year-old male with lentigo maligna melanoma (LMM) on the scalp. The overall Breslow thickness for this case was 1.4 mm.
cleaned: Melanoma with atypical melanocytes exhibiting pleomorphism and hyperchromatic nuclei. Lesion displays irregular nest patterns and pagetoid spread. Tissue architecture shows increased dermal melanocytes.

label: melanoma
original: Microscopic image of histopathology for Case 31, a 79-year-old male with lentigo maligna melanoma (LMM) on the scalp. The image, stained with H&E and viewed at 100x magnification, shows evidence of lymphovascular invasion (LVI) on Level 4 of examination. In this specific case, the presence of LVI did not result in a change in the pT stage. The overall Breslow thickness for this case was 1.4 mm.
cleaned: Irregular nests of atypical melanocytes with prominent nucleoli extending into the dermis. Scattered single atypical melanocytes are present. Lymphatic spaces contain tumor cells.

label: melanoma
original: Hematoxylin and eosin (H&E) stained histopathology showing dermal melanophages with viable melanoma cells (arrowhead), suggestive of partial regression. Tumoral melanosis (TM) is a histological term describing aggregations of melanin-containing macrophages (melanophages) often found in the dermis, typically devoid of accompanying melanocytes, and is strongly associated with primary and/or metastatic melanoma.
cleaned: Dermal melanophages and viable melanoma cells. Aggregations of melanin-containing macrophages in the dermis. Melanoma.

label: melanoma
original: Hematoxylin and eosin (H&E) stained histopathology showing dermal melanophages with viable melanoma cells (arrowhead), suggestive of partial regression. Tumoral melanosis (TM) is a histological term describing aggregations of melanin-containing macrophages (melanophages) often found in the dermis, typically devoid of accompanying melanocytes, and is strongly associated with primary and/or metastatic melanoma.
cleaned: Dermal melanophages and viable melanoma cells. Aggregations of melanin-containing macrophages in the dermis. Melanoma.

label: nevus
original: Histopathology of a 5-µm section from formalin-fixed, paraffin-embedded tissue, stained with Hematoxylin and Eosin (H&E) at ×100 magnification, showing a deep penetrating nevus. Nests of spindle cells and epithelioid-like cells are observed as deep dermal and subcutaneous nested proliferations, arranged in a wedge-shaped pattern. These nests diffusely penetrate and infiltrate the surrounding tissues, including the deep subcutaneous adipose tissue on the left. Note the abundance of melanophages along with some pigment incontinence.
cleaned: Nests of spindle cells and epithelioid-like cells are observed as deep dermal and subcutaneous nested proliferations, arranged in a wedge-shaped pattern. These nests diffusely penetrate and infiltrate the surrounding tissues, including the deep subcutaneous adipose tissue. Abundance of melanophages and pigment incontinence are noted. Deep penetrating nevus.

label: nevus
original: Histopathology (H&E, ×20 magnification) showing nested proliferations of spindle and epithelioid cells within the deep reticular dermis and subcutaneous tissue. These cells are arranged in a characteristic wedge shape with a broad base in parallel with the deep reticular dermis and an apex directed toward the epidermis. This lesion, subsequently diagnosed as a deep penetrating nevus, features nests that diffusely penetrate and infiltrate the surrounding tissues. The tumor cells exhibit severe cytologic atypia and pleomorphism, along with occasional nuclear vacuoles and large pseudoinclusions. Abundant melanophages and some pigment incontinence are also present.
cleaned: Nested proliferations of spindle and epithelioid cells within the deep reticular dermis and subcutaneous tissue. Cells are arranged in a wedge shape with a broad base and an apex directed toward the epidermis. Nests diffusely penetrate and infiltrate surrounding tissues. Tumor cells exhibit cytologic atypia and pleomorphism, with nuclear vacuoles and large pseudoinclusions. Abundant melanophages and pigment incontinence are present. Deep penetrating nevus.

label: nevus
original: Histopathology of a deep penetrating nevus, observed in 5-um sections from formalin-fixed, paraffin-embedded tissue stained with Hematoxylin and Eosin (H&E) at ×400 magnification. The image displays tumor cells revealing severe cytologic atypia and pleomorphism. Several cells exhibit nuclear vacuolation, with occasional large pseudoinclusions also noted. The overall lesion showed deep dermal and subcutaneous nested proliferations of spindle and epithelioid cells arranged in a wedge-shaped pattern, with nests diffusely penetrating and infiltrating the surrounding tissues, along with abundant melanophages and some pigment incontinence.
cleaned: Deep dermal and subcutaneous nested proliferation of spindle and epithelioid cells arranged in a wedge-shaped pattern, infiltrating surrounding tissues. Cells exhibit severe cytologic atypia, pleomorphism, nuclear vacuolation, and occasional large pseudoinclusions. Abundant melanophages and pigment incontinence are present. Deep penetrating nevus.

label: melanoma
original: This is a low-power histological view (hematoxylin and eosin stain, × 80 magnification) depicting the typical histological morphology of a polypoid malignant melanoma (MM) of the anorectal region, characterized by superficial ulceration. This represents primary anorectal melanoma, which is considered when the tumour arises in the rectum, anal canal, or anal margin.
cleaned: Polypoid lesion with superficial ulceration. Malignant melanoma.

label: melanoma
original: This detailed histological view (hematoxylin and eosin stain, × 250 magnification) shows extensive tumour growth of anorectal melanoma beneath an intact surface squamous epithelium. This represents a typical histological morphology of primary anorectal melanoma, a condition where the tumour arises in the rectum, anal canal, or anal margin.
cleaned: Extensive tumor growth beneath intact surface squamous epithelium. Tumor cells exhibit typical morphology of anorectal melanoma. Diagnosis: melanoma.

label: melanoma
original: Histopathology of the enucleated right eye from a 79-year-old man with a history of age-related macular degeneration, 9 months after local resection of a minimally pigmented ciliary body/choroidal mixed cell type melanoma. The enucleated eye showed no residual melanoma. This image highlights the area posterior to the resected ciliary body, where the choroid terminates (indicated by an arrow) in the region of the previously resected tumor. Detached, gliotic retina (labeled R) is present in this area. The tissue is stained with Hematoxylin and Eosin at 25x magnification.
cleaned:
Detached, gliotic retina is present posterior to the resected ciliary body, where the choroid terminates. 

Diagnosis: melanoma.

label: melanoma
original: Histopathology of skin showing malignant melanoma (Clark's level V, Breslow thickness 45 mm) with infiltrating melanin pigment containing epithelioid and spindle tumor cells. cleaned: Malignant melanoma with infiltrating melanin pigment containing epithelioid and spindle tumor cells. Brisk mitoses and junctional activity are present.

label: melanoma
original: Photomicrograph showing histopathological features of an excised orbital mass, consistent with a mixed spindle cell and epitheloid cell melanoma. Melanin is absent in the tumor cells, consistent with the largely amelanotic nature of this melanoma. (H&E, ×400 magnification)
cleaned: Sheet of plump epitheloid tumor cells with large nuclei and prominent nucleoli. Melanin is absent in the tumor cells. Melanoma.

label: melanoma
original: Histopathology of a biopsy specimen from a 56-year-old man with flat and black pigmented mucosal lesions in the distal esophagus and esophagogastric junction, showing primary malignant melanoma of the esophagus (PMME). The tumor mainly consists of poorly cohesive neoplastic cells with hyperchromatic nuclei and cytosolic melanin granules, which predominantly proliferated in the mucosa. Hematoxylin and eosin stain, ×400 magnification.
cleaned: Poorly cohesive neoplastic cells with hyperchromatic nuclei and cytosolic melanin granules proliferating within the mucosa. Primary malignant melanoma of the esophagus.

label: nevus
original: Histopathologic findings of a dark brown, dome-shaped mass excised from the posterior wall of the left ear canal's cartilaginous portion in a 33-year-old woman. The image is stained with Hematoxylin-eosin, original magnification ×200.
cleaned: Dark brown, dome-shaped mass. Scattered clusters of round to oval-shaped melanocytic cells located in the dermis. Atypical melanocytes without pleomorphism. Dysplastic nevus within an intradermal nevus.

label: nevus
original: Histopathologic findings of the mass excised from the left ear canal of a 33-year-old woman, showing numerous nests and groups of melanocytic nevus cells located in the dermis, covered with normal skin. These findings were compatible with an intradermal nevus, but the nevus contained atypical melanocytes without pleomorphism, leading to a final diagnosis of a dysplastic nevus within an intradermal nevus (Hematoxylin-eosin, original magnification ×10).
cleaned: Nests and groups of melanocytic cells within the dermis, covered by normal skin. Cells are without pleomorphism. Diagnosis: dysplastic nevus within an intradermal nevus.

label: nevus
original: Histopathologic appearance showing a junctional nevus in an 8-year-old boy. The image is stained with hematoxylin and eosin (H&E) and captured at ×200 magnification.
cleaned: Junctional nevus. Clusters of melanocytes at the dermal-epidermal junction. Uniform, round to oval melanocytes with minimal cytologic atypia. Normal overlying epidermis.

label: nevus
original: Histopathologic appearance of a blue nevus, diagnosed in an adult and located at the caruncle, showing proliferating stromal melanocytes. Stained with hematoxylin and eosin (×200 magnification).
cleaned: Proliferating stromal melanocytes. Blue nevus.

label: nevus
original: Histopathologic appearance of a compound nevus, stained with hematoxylin and eosin, at ×100 magnification.
cleaned: Compound nevus. Junctional melanocytes and dermal nevus cells are present, exhibiting uniform, round to oval nuclei with moderate cytoplasm. The lesion demonstrates a symmetrical architecture with well-defined borders.

label: nevus
original: Histopathology showing a subepithelial cystic nevus (hematoxylin and eosin, original magnification ×200). In this study, subepithelial nevi comprised 24% (n=25) of the excised lesions, and 36% (9/25) of these subepithelial nevi were observed to be cystic. Overall, 60% of all nevi in the study were cystic.
cleaned: Subepithelial cysts with a nested architecture. Cells are uniform with round nuclei. Diagnosis: cystic nevus.

label: nevus
original: Histopathologic appearance of a compound nevus, stained with hematoxylin and eosin (H&E) and viewed at ×200 magnification.
cleaned:
Nests of melanocytes within the epidermis and dermis. The lesion demonstrates a symmetrical architecture with maturation of nevus cells with descent. Uniform, round to oval melanocytes with small nucleoli are present. 

Diagnosis: Nevus

label: nevus
original: Histopathology showing a subepithelial nevus located at the caruncle in a 14-year-old boy. This image is an example of a subepithelial nevus, a diagnosis that comprised 24% (n=25) of the 105 excised lesions in the study. All three nevi located at the caruncle in the study were subepithelial. The section is stained with hematoxylin and eosin (×200 magnification).
cleaned: Nests of melanocytes within the dermis. The melanocytes exhibit uniform, round nuclei and moderate cytoplasm. The overlying epidermis is unremarkable. Diagnosis: Nevus.

label: nevus
original: Histopathologic appearance of a caruncular subepithelial nevus from this patient, stained with hematoxylin and eosin (H&E) at ×200 magnification.
cleaned:
Nodular, well-circumscribed lesion with symmetrical architecture. Predominantly composed of melanocytes arranged in nests. Cells exhibit moderate pigmentation and uniform morphology. 

Diagnosis: Nevus

label: melanoma
original: Photomicrograph showing malignant melanoma cells arranged in an alveolar pattern. Histologically, the tumour was composed predominantly of epithelioid tumour cells. The diagnosis of malignant melanoma was suggested by characteristic nesting of malignant cells beneath the bronchial epithelium with junctional change and mitotic activity, and was confirmed by positive immunohistochemical reactions for S-100 protein and melanoma associated monoclonal antibody HMB45. (Hematoxylin-Eosin stain, ×400 magnification).
cleaned: Malignant melanoma cells arranged in an alveolar pattern. Predominantly epithelioid tumor cells. Nesting of malignant cells beneath the epithelium with junctional change and mitotic activity. Malignant melanoma.

label: melanoma
original: Photomicrograph of a polypoidal mass of malignant melanoma, showing an endobronchial tumor with mucosal invasion. Histologically, the tumor is composed predominantly of epithelioid tumor cells, exhibiting junctional change and mitotic activity, with characteristic nesting of malignant cells beneath the bronchial epithelium, features suggestive of malignant melanoma. (Hematoxylin-Eosin ×50).
cleaned: Polypoidal mass of malignant melanoma with mucosal invasion. Tumor composed of epithelioid cells exhibiting junctional change and mitotic activity, with nesting of malignant cells beneath the epithelium.

label: melanoma
original: Photomicrograph of the central cyst of the mass lesion, revealing blood-filled, nonendothelial lined pseudovascular spaces. These pseudovascular spaces are surrounded and lined by dendritic melanocytes (lesional cells), a finding analogous to an angiomatoid melanoma or aneurysmal benign fibrous histiocytoma. Immunohistochemical analysis of these tumor cells demonstrated positivity for HMB-45, S-100, and melan-A, and negativity for estrogen receptor, progesterone receptor, CD34, smooth muscle actin, and AE1/AE3, with a MIB-1 proliferative index of less than 1%. (Hematoxylin and eosin stain, original magnification, × 40).
cleaned: Central cyst with blood-filled, nonendothelial lined pseudovascular spaces. Pseudovascular spaces are surrounded and lined by dendritic melanocytes. Angiomatoid melanoma or aneurysmal benign fibrous histiocytoma.

label: nevus
original: Histopathology photomicrograph showing nevus cells with junctional activity in the epidermodermal junction and extending into the dermis, accompanied by excessive chronic inflammatory cell infiltration. The image is stained with Hematoxylin and Eosin (H&E) and captured at ×100 magnification.
cleaned:
Nevus cells with junctional activity at the epidermodermal junction extending into the dermis, accompanied by chronic inflammatory cell infiltration. 

Diagnosis: Nevus.

label: nevus
original: Photomicrograph depicting a cystic nevus. The image is stained with Hematoxylin and Eosin (H&E) and captured at ×25 magnification.
cleaned: Cystic nevus.

label: nevus
original: Photomicrograph illustrating a junctional nevus, characterized by nevus cells situated at the epidermodermal junction. The image is stained with Hematoxylin and Eosin (H&E) and captured at ×25 magnification.
cleaned: Nevus cells at the epidermodermal junction. Junctional nevus.

label: nevus
original: Histopathology demonstrating incidental epidermolytic hyperkeratosis (EHK) occurring in association with a dysplastic junctional nevus. This represents one of three cases of EHK identified in the study that were associated with biopsies of dysplastic/Clark's nevi. The EHK lesions in these cases had an average diameter of 0.15 mm, with a range of 0.05 mm to 0.8 mm. The image is presented at 200X magnification and stained with hematoxylin and eosin.
cleaned: Epidermolytic hyperkeratosis with a dysplastic junctional nevus. The lesion demonstrates hyperkeratosis.

label: melanoma
original: Histopathology showing incidental focal acantholytic dyskeratosis (FAD) associated with prominent solar elastosis. This specific FAD case, one of three FAD patterns identified in the study, was found in a skin specimen that occurred in association with an excision of a melanoma in situ. This is one of four cases in the study associated with prominent solar elastosis and one of three cases associated with malignant or premalignant epidermal neoplasia (melanoma in situ with FAD). The average diameter of acantholysis observed in such cases was 0.3 mm, with a range of 0.1 mm to 0.5 mm. Patients with incidental FAD had an average age of 68 years (range 41–86). (400X magnification, hematoxylin, and eosin stain).
cleaned: Focal areas of cells with loss of intercellular adhesion and irregular keratinization. Prominent amorphous, eosinophilic material in the dermis. Scattered atypical melanocytes within the epidermis.

label: nevus
original: High-power photomicrograph showing pathologic findings consistent with congenital melanocytic nevi, stained with hematoxylin and eosin (H&E). The image displays the keratinized, stratified squamous epithelium (thick black arrow) and the epidermodermal junction (thick white arrow). The epidermis is thinned out with loss of rete pegs. The underlying dermis shows diffuse deposition of melanin pigment (thin white arrow) and the presence of melanocytes (thin black arrow) around and within the hair follicles. These findings, observed during microscopic examination of the patient's scalp swelling, also included lobules and nests of nevi cells, hair follicles, sweat glands, and sebaceous glands, with melanin pigment and melanocytes extending up to the deep subcutis and infiltrating the fat, all indicative of congenital melanocytic nevi.
cleaned: Keratinized, stratified squamous epithelium and epidermodermal junction. Thinned epidermis with loss of rete pegs. Diffuse melanin pigment deposition and melanocytes around and within hair follicles. Lobules and nests of nevi cells, hair follicles, sweat glands, and sebaceous glands. Melanin pigment and melanocytes extending into deep subcutis and infiltrating fat. Congenital melanocytic nevi.

label: melanoma
original: Histopathology showing a symmetrical, well-circumscribed papule with a fibro-mucinous stroma containing spindle-shaped and dendritic melanocytes, which sometimes were arranged in whorls. Melanophages are present. These features are consistent with the diagnosis of a sclerosing mucinous blue naevus and help exclude malignant melanoma. Magnification 20x, Hematoxylin and Eosin stain.
cleaned: Symmetrical, well-circumscribed papule with a fibro-mucinous stroma containing spindle-shaped and dendritic melanocytes, sometimes arranged in whorls. Melanophages are present. Sclerosing mucinous blue naevus.

label: melanoma
original: Photomicrograph showing the course of a perianal fistulous tract with a nest of melanocytic cells, consistent with a primary fistula melanoma. This lesion was located in the depth of the subcutaneous tissue, adjacent to the epithelialized fistulous tract, and surrounded by abundant inflammatory response. The pathologist's examination of the resected specimen revealed an abscessed fistulous tract associated with a small core of hyperpigmented, hyperplastic basal melanocytes arranged irregularly in a lentiginous pattern, and a discernible nest of epithelioid cells with atypical anaplastic nuclei, prominent nucleoli, and abundant melanin in the underlying dermis. Hematoxylin-Eosin staining; original magnification 4×.
cleaned: Fistulous tract with a nest of melanocytic cells. Hyperpigmented, hyperplastic basal melanocytes arranged irregularly. Nest of epithelioid cells with atypical nuclei, prominent nucleoli, and abundant melanin in the dermis. Inflammatory response. Diagnosis: melanoma.

label: melanoma
original: Photomicrograph providing a closer view of the melanocytic cell core within the perianal fistula melanoma. This core consists of epithelioid cells with atypical anaplastic nuclei, prominent nucleoli, and abundant melanin, forming an irregular, lentiginous pattern within the underlying dermis next to the fistulous tract. The presence of these atypical cells and abundant melanic pigment throughout the lesion is characteristic of melanoma. Hematoxylin-Eosin staining; original magnification 10×.
cleaned: Epithelioid cells with atypical anaplastic nuclei, prominent nucleoli, and abundant melanin forming an irregular, lentiginous pattern within the dermis. Melanoma.

label: melanoma
original: Photomicrograph showing individual melanoma cancerous cells at high magnification, including one cell clearly undergoing mitosis. These epithelioid cells exhibit atypical anaplastic nuclei, prominent nucleoli, and abundant melanin. The presence of mitoses in deep cells is a key feature relatively specific for melanoma, supporting the diagnosis of a primary fistula melanoma identified incidentally in a 38-year-old male. Hematoxylin-Eosin staining; original magnification 40×.
cleaned: Epithelioid cells with atypical anaplastic nuclei, prominent nucleoli, and abundant melanin. One cell undergoing mitosis is visible. Melanoma.

label: melanoma
original: Photomicrographic image of a cross-section of the entire resected fistula piece, stained with Hematoxylin and Eosin (H&E) at 2× magnification. This image shows the fistulous tract, the lentiginous melanocytic hyperplasia, and the perianal skin epidermis. The melanoma, which was quite small and found incidentally during histological examination, is located in the depth of the subcutaneous tissue, next to the fistulous tract, the interior of which was already epithelialised. The melanoma is surrounded by an abundant inflammatory response and is next to the visible lesion showing atypical lentiginous melanocytic hyperplasia. The presence of lentiginous hyperplasia suggested that the melanocytic lesion originated inside the fistulous tract, supporting the diagnosis of a primary melanoma. The opening of the fistulous tract in the perianal skin epidermis does not contain signs of inflammation or malignant tissue, ruling out the possibility of the fistula being a complication or a secondary manifestation of a cutaneous melanoma.
cleaned:
Fistulous tract with epithelialized interior. Lentiginous melanocytic hyperplasia visible in perianal skin epidermis and adjacent to the tract. Small melanoma located in the depth of subcutaneous tissue next to the fistulous tract, surrounded by inflammatory cells. Atypical lentiginous melanocytic hyperplasia adjacent to the melanoma.

Diagnosis: melanoma, lentiginous melanocytic hyperplasia.

label: melanoma
original: Low-power image of cutaneous melanoma in situ, stained with hematoxylin and eosin (H&E). Melanocytes in melanoma in situ frequently fail to possess a primary cilium. Scale bar: 100 µm.
cleaned: Melanocytes with atypical morphology are present within the epidermis. Diagnosis: melanoma in situ.

label: nevus
original: Low-power image of a cutaneous melanocytic nevus, where primary cilia are typically retained by the melanocytes. This image is stained with hematoxylin and eosin (H&E), showing the general histopathology of the nevus. Scale bar: 100 µm.
cleaned: Cutaneous melanocytic nevus with retained primary cilia on melanocytes. The tissue shows nests of melanocytes.

label: melanoma
original: Low-power image of invasive cutaneous melanoma, stained with hematoxylin and eosin. The study investigates melanocytes in invasive melanoma, which frequently fail to possess a primary cilium. Scale bar: 100 µm.
cleaned: Invasive cutaneous melanoma. Irregular nests of atypical melanocytes with prominent nucleoli infiltrate the dermis. Cells exhibit variable morphology and hyperchromatic nuclei.

label: melanoma
original: Light microscopic findings of the surgical specimen revealing large epithelioid tumor cells with dusty brown melanin pigments and frequent mitotic figures. These features are consistent with the diagnosis of primary malignant melanoma of the urethra. (H&E, ×400).
cleaned: Large epithelioid tumor cells with dusty brown melanin pigments and frequent mitotic figures. Malignant melanoma.

label: melanoma
original: Histopathology of an esophageal tumor biopsy showing a proliferation of epithelioid and spindle-shaped cells arranged in fascicles, with the presence of melanin pigment. This tumor, located in the junction medium-distal esophagus, was identified as a 2 cm, lobulated, and pigmented mass, leading to a diagnosis of primary esophageal melanoma. Hematoxylin and eosin (HE) stain, original magnification ×40.
cleaned: Proliferation of epithelioid and spindle-shaped cells arranged in fascicles, with melanin pigment. Lobulated, pigmented mass. Esophageal melanoma.

label: melanoma
original: Microscopic image of a bladder polyp biopsy from a 54-year-old male patient, revealing a diffuse, dense proliferation of small to medium-sized neoplastic plasmacytoid cells. The neoplastic cells demonstrate eosinophilic cytoplasm and eccentric nuclei with prominent nucleoli. Some cells are binucleated (indicated by '⇒'). Note the presence of melanin pigment (indicated by '⊷') within some cells. This morphology, in the context of the patient's presentation and subsequent immunohistochemistry, led to a diagnosis of metastatic plasmacytoid melanoma. Stained with Hematoxylin and Eosin (HE), magnification ×400.
cleaned: Diffuse, dense proliferation of small to medium-sized neoplastic plasmacytoid cells with eosinophilic cytoplasm and eccentric nuclei with prominent nucleoli. Some cells are binucleated. Melanin pigment is present within some cells. Plasmacytoid melanoma.

label: melanoma
original: Histopathology of a bladder polyp, showing a diffuse and dense proliferation of rounded, neoplastic cells with plasmacytoid morphology. The cells are small to medium, characterized by eosinophilic cytoplasm and eccentric nuclei with prominent central nucleoli. The neoplastic cells display incohesion within the tissue. This finding is consistent with metastatic plasmacytoid melanoma. Hematoxylin and eosin stain, original magnification ×40.
cleaned: Diffuse and dense proliferation of rounded, neoplastic cells with plasmacytoid morphology. Cells are small to medium, characterized by eosinophilic cytoplasm and eccentric nuclei with prominent central nucleoli. Neoplastic cells display incohesion within the tissue. Plasmacytoid melanoma.

label: nevus
original: Histopathology showing clusters of nevus cells containing melanin pigment. These nevus cells are characterized by a large nucleus and scanty eosinophilic cytoplasm. This image is from a case diagnosed as osteonevus of Nanta (or osseous metaplasia in benign intradermal melanocytic nevus). (H&E, 40x).
cleaned: Clusters of nevus cells containing melanin pigment. Nevus cells are characterized by a large nucleus and scanty eosinophilic cytoplasm. Osseous metaplasia in benign intradermal melanocytic nevus.

label: melanoma

label: melanoma

label: melanoma

This image illustrates the morphology of melanoma within the urinary bladder, representing metastasis in a patient with a known history of malignant melanoma. label: melanoma

label: nevus
original: Histopathology of a lesional biopsy, consistent with bilateral, systematized epidermolytic verrucous epidermal nevus, showing massive hyperkeratosis and focal granular degeneration of the epidermis. label: melanoma
original: Low-power photomicrograph of primary penile desmoplastic melanoma, stained with Hematoxylin and Eosin (H&E). The image depicts the penile glans, showing haphazardly arranged and disarrayed spindle tumor cells permeating in between abundant collagen bundles, which comprise over 90% of the entire neoplasm. These cells deeply infiltrate the dermis and corpus spongiosum, exhibiting conspicuous neurotropism. In intimate association with the spindle tumor cells, focal atypical intraepidermal melanocytic proliferation and pseudoepitheliomatous hyperplasia are observed in the overlying epidermis. Histologically, moderate nuclear pleomorphism and rare atypical mitoses are also characteristic features of this tumor.

Focal atypical melanocytic proliferation and pseudoepitheliomatous hyperplasia are observed in the overlying epidermis. Moderate nuclear pleomorphism and rare atypical mitoses are present.

Diagnosis: Desmoplastic melanoma.

cleaned: Compound melanocytic neoplasm with clear-cut congenital-like features. Acral compound melanocytic nevus with congenital features.

label: nevus
original: Histopathology (40× hematoxylin & eosin [H&E] stain) showing the central area of the lesion, which is mainly an intradermal combination of type C and dendritic (blue nevus-like) melanocytes. The final histopathological diagnosis was acral compound melanocytic nevus with congenital features.
cleaned: Intradermal lesion composed of type C and dendritic melanocytes. Acral compound melanocytic nevus with congenital features.

label: nevus
original: Histopathology (40× hematoxylin & eosin [H&E] stain) illustrating a striking acrosyringial and periductal distribution of the melanocytic neoplasm. The final histopathological diagnosis was acral compound melanocytic nevus with congenital features.
cleaned: Melanocytic neoplasm with acrosyringial and periductal distribution. Acral compound melanocytic nevus with congenital features.

label: nevus
original: Histopathology (20× hematoxylin & eosin [H&E] stain) demonstrating a junctional component that is mainly peripheral, with regularly arranged melanocytic nests at the dermo-epidermal junction without cytologic atypia. The final histopathological diagnosis was acral compound melanocytic nevus with congenital features.
cleaned: Junctional component mainly peripheral, with regularly arranged melanocytic nests at the dermo-epidermal junction without cytologic atypia. Areas with melanin columns from melanocytes located in the crista profunda intermedia are visible. Acral compound melanocytic nevus with congenital features.

label: nevus
original: Histopathology (40× hematoxylin & eosin [H&E] stain) depicting columnar melanin depositions (melanin columns) arrayed under the surface furrow. The final histopathological diagnosis was acral compound melanocytic nevus with congenital features.
cleaned: Columnar melanin depositions arrayed under the surface furrow, also observed in the crista profunda intermedia under the surface ridge. Acral compound melanocytic nevus with congenital features.

label: melanoma
original: Histopathological examination showing a proliferative growth centered in the upper dermis with marked thinning of the epidermis. cleaned: Proliferative growth centered in the upper dermis with marked thinning of the epidermis. Malignant melanoma.

label: melanoma
original: Histopathological examination (HPE) showing a mass composed of clustered polygonal tumor cells. The cells exhibit hyperchromatic nuclei, perinuclear halos, prominent nucleoli, inclusion bodies, and conspicuous mitotic activity. Melanin pigment was frequently seen, and separate cell nests and tumor emboli were identified. These features are suggestive of malignant melanoma. (Hematoxylin and Eosin stain, ×400 magnification)
cleaned: Clustered polygonal tumor cells with hyperchromatic nuclei, perinuclear halos, prominent nucleoli, inclusion bodies, and conspicuous mitotic activity. Melanin pigment is frequently seen. Separate cell nests and tumor emboli are identified. Malignant melanoma.

label: melanoma

cleaned: Pagetoid appearance with atypical melanocytes. Diagnosis: superficial spreading melanoma.

label: nevus
original: Histological examination showing proliferation of nevus cells and melanocytes visible on the tip of the rete ridges. Stained with Hematoxylin and Eosin (H&E) at ×40 magnification.
cleaned:
Proliferation of nevus cells and melanocytes visible on the tip of the rete ridges.

Diagnosis: Nevus.

label: melanoma
original: Microscopic histopathology of anorectal melanoma (H&E staining, ×400 magnification) showing tumor containing highly anaplastic, discohesive cells without a specific growth pattern.
