Superficial spreading melanoma is a slow-growing melanoma characterized by an initial radial growth phase (RGP) with epidermal involvement, followed by a vertical growth phase.

Superficial spreading melanoma.

label: melanoma
original: Histopathology image of a superficial spreading melanoma (T1a, Breslow 0.5 mm) on the abdomen of an 82-year-old man, stained with Hematoxylin and Eosin (HE) at 100× original magnification. This higher magnification image shows atypical melanocytes in single units and irregular nests mostly arranged at the dermo-epidermal junction (DEJ), along with melanophages in the dermis. These features correlate with the structureless bichromatic brown-blue areas (labeled +) seen in dermoscopy. Superficial spreading melanoma is a biologically slow-growing melanoma, typically showing an initial radial growth phase involving the epidermis and DEJ, followed by a vertical growth phase (VGP) with invasion into the dermis.
cleaned: Atypical melanocytes in single units and irregular nests mostly arranged at the dermo-epidermal junction. Melanophages are present in the dermis. Brown-blue areas are visible. Superficial spreading melanoma.

label: melanoma
original: Histopathology (Hematoxylin and Eosin, HE, ×100) of melanoma in situ on the left flank of a 62-year-old man. The image shows a proliferation of atypical melanocytes, present in single units and nests, extending through all layers of the epidermis. Additionally, a dense lymphohistiocytic infiltrate with melanophages is visible in the dermis.
cleaned: Proliferation of atypical melanocytes in single units and nests extending through all layers of the epidermis. Dense lymphohistiocytic infiltrate with melanophages visible in the dermis. Melanoma in situ.

label: melanoma
original: Histopathology (×200) showing the correlation of dermoscopic features with histological findings in melanoma in situ. The image displays irregular pigmented areas that correlate with clumps of melanin and pigmented parakeratosis. Irregular hyperpigmented areas are among the most prevalent dermatoscopic features in small diameter melanoma (<5 mm) and typically correlate with clumps of melanin or hyperpigmented atypical melanocytes in the epidermis or stratum corneum. The structureless brown areas observed dermoscopically correlate with a dense lymphohistiocytic infiltrate with melanophages in the dermis. The staining is likely Hematoxylin and Eosin (H&E), given the context of the overall histological analysis.
cleaned:
Irregular pigmented areas with clumps of melanin and pigmented parakeratosis. Irregular hyperpigmented areas with atypical melanocytes in the epidermis and stratum corneum. Structureless brown areas correlating with a dense lymphohistiocytic infiltrate with melanophages in the dermis. 

Melanoma in situ.

label: melanoma
original: Histopathology of a superficial spreading melanoma (T1a, Breslow 0.53 mm) from the left chest of a 44-year-old man. Superficial spreading melanoma is characterized by an initial radial growth phase (RGP) with atypical melanocytes. This hematoxylin and eosin (HE) stained image (100× magnification) shows peripheral, confluent, and heavily pigmented atypical junctional nests of melanocytes. These nests correlate with pseudopods observed in dermoscopy. Additionally, structureless black and blue areas seen in dermoscopy are typically related to a heavy band-like lymphohistiocytic infiltrate with melanophages in the dermis.
cleaned:
Atypical melanocytes form peripheral, confluent, and heavily pigmented nests at the dermal-epidermal junction. A dense, band-like lymphohistiocytic infiltrate with melanophages is present in the dermis.
Superficial spreading melanoma.

label: melanoma
original: Histopathology of a superficial spreading melanoma (T1a, Breslow 0.53 mm) from the left chest of a 44-year-old man. Superficial spreading melanoma is characterized by an initial radial growth phase (RGP) with atypical melanocytes at the dermoepidermal junction. This hematoxylin and eosin (HE) stained image (100× magnification) reveals irregularly large atypical nests of melanocytes at the dermoepidermal junction (DEJ), which correspond to irregular globules observed in dermoscopy.
cleaned: Irregularly large atypical nests of melanocytes at the dermoepidermal junction. Superficial spreading melanoma.

label: melanoma
original: Histopathology of a superficial spreading melanoma (T1a, Breslow 0.53 mm) from the left chest of a 44-year-old man. Superficial spreading melanoma is characterized by an initial radial growth phase (RGP) with atypical melanocytes proliferating in the epidermis. This hematoxylin and eosin (HE) stained image (100× magnification) demonstrates small atypical nests of melanocytes or irregular clumps of melanin within the epidermis. These histological features correlate with irregular dots observed in dermoscopy.
cleaned: Atypical nests of melanocytes and irregular clumps of melanin within the epidermis. Melanoma.

label: melanoma
original: Histopathology showing atypical melanocytes forming the “shoulder” of a malignant melanoma lesion. The atypical melanocytes ascend to the level of the dermoepidermal junction and show a tendency to invade the superficial and middle dermis. These elements are frankly of a melanocytic nature, with occasional pigment. This “junctional” component is also characterized by being strongly positive for S-100 protein, Melan-A, and HMB-45, but negative for CD10, as determined by immunohistochemical examination. (Hematoxylin-Eosin, Original Magnification: 10×).
cleaned: Atypical melanocytes forming a lesion with cells ascending to the dermoepidermal junction and invading the superficial and middle dermis. Melanocytes with occasional pigment. Malignant melanoma.

label: melanoma
original: Histological micrograph highlighting the two morphologically different components of dedifferentiated melanoma. These include elements of a melanocytic nature, which invade the superficial and middle dermis, and an exophytic polypoid nodule consisting of pleomorphic, atypical elements. On immunohistochemical examination, the melanocytic component (the “shoulder” of the lesion) is strongly positive for S-100 protein, Melan-A, and HMB-45, but negative for CD10. In contrast, the nodular component is almost entirely negative for S-100, Melan-A, and HMB-45, only focally positive for SOX-10, and strongly positive for CD10. (Hematoxylin–Eosin, original magnification: 20×).
cleaned: Melanocytic cells invading the superficial and middle dermis, and an exophytic polypoid nodule consisting of pleomorphic, atypical elements. Dedifferentiated melanoma.

label: melanoma
original: Histopathology showing melanoma in situ. The image is stained with Hematoxylin and eosin at 20x magnification.
cleaned: Melanoma in situ. Atypical melanocytes are present within the epidermis, exhibiting large nuclei and prominent nucleoli. The lesion displays irregular borders and pagetoid spread of melanocytes.

label: nevus
original: Histopathology of a conventional deep penetrating nevus. A wedge-shaped (inverted triangle) proliferation of enlarged fusiform, spindled, and/or epithelioid melanocytes is observed extending into the deep dermis and subcutis, with an overlying grenz zone. The lesion is typically small, well-circumscribed, and symmetrical. (Hematoxylin and eosin, 20× magnification).
cleaned: Wedge-shaped proliferation of enlarged fusiform, spindled, and epithelioid melanocytes extending into the deep dermis and subcutis, with an overlying grenz zone. The lesion is small, well-circumscribed, and symmetrical. Deep penetrating nevus.

label: nevus
original: Histopathology of a conventional deep penetrating nevus. The lesional melanocytes, which are typically enlarged fusiform, spindled, and/or epithelioid, are arranged in characteristic fascicles, cords, and nests. These bundles of melanocytes may be associated with neurovascular and adnexal structures, giving a plexiform appearance. (Hematoxylin and eosin, 100× magnification).
cleaned: Lesional melanocytes are enlarged, fusiform, spindled, and/or epithelioid, arranged in fascicles, cords, and nests. Bundles of melanocytes are associated with neurovascular and adnexal structures, giving a plexiform appearance. Deep penetrating nevus.

label: nevus
original: Histopathology of a conventional deep penetrating nevus. The melanocytes are epithelioid and spindled, possessing amphophilic cytoplasm. Admixed are scattered, often sparse to abundant, heavily pigmented melanophages. A lymphocytic infiltrate may also be present. There is no associated necrosis or loss or invasion of adnexal structures. (Hematoxylin and eosin, 100× magnification).
cleaned: Epithelioid and spindled melanocytes with amphophilic cytoplasm. Scattered, often sparse to abundant, heavily pigmented melanophages are admixed. Lymphocytic infiltrate may be present. No associated necrosis or loss of invasion of adnexal structures. Deep penetrating nevus.

label: nevus
original: Histopathology showing a deep penetrating nevus associated with a predominantly intradermal melanocytic nevus, presented as part of a combined nevus. Deep penetrating nevi often exist in this combined form, frequently associated with other nevoid subtypes like conventional, blue, or Spitz nevi. Stained with hematoxylin and eosin, 40× magnification.
cleaned: Deep penetrating nevus with predominantly intradermal melanocytic nevus, presented as a combined nevus. Melanocytic cells are present.

label: nevus
original: Histopathology showing a deep penetrating nevus associated with a predominantly intradermal melanocytic nevus, presented as part of a combined nevus. Deep penetrating nevi often exist in this combined form, frequently associated with other nevoid subtypes like conventional, blue, or Spitz nevi. Stained with hematoxylin and eosin, 100× magnification.
cleaned: Deep penetrating nevus with predominantly intradermal melanocytic nevus, presented as a combined nevus. Melanocytic cells are present.

label: nevus
original: Histopathology of an atypical deep penetrating nevus originating from the eyelid margin, stained with Hematoxylin and eosin at 40× magnification. This panel provides an overview of the lesion, which exhibits features consistent with atypical deep penetrating nevi.
cleaned:
Asymmetric proliferation of melanocytes within the dermis, with large, irregular nests and fascicles. Cells exhibit marked nuclear pleomorphism and hyperchromasia. The lesion demonstrates deep penetration into the reticular dermis. 
Diagnosis: Atypical deep penetrating nevus.

label: nevus
original: Histopathology of an atypical deep penetrating nevus from the eyelid margin, stained with Hematoxylin and eosin at 200× magnification. The image reveals moderate cytologic atypia and rare mitotic figures, which are characteristic findings in atypical deep penetrating nevi.
cleaned: Moderate cytologic atypia and rare mitotic figures are visible. Atypical deep penetrating nevus.

label: nevus
original: Histopathology of an atypical deep penetrating nevus (DPN) at 200x magnification, stained with hematoxylin and eosin. This panel highlights the junctional component, which is predominantly nested and epithelioid. Occasional pagetoid upward migration of atypical single melanocytes is present. The atypical cells within the junctional component are cytologically similar to those seen in the dermis and may exhibit moderate to severe cytologic atypia, including pleomorphism, high nuclear to cytoplasmic ratios, and conspicuous cherry-red nucleoli.
cleaned: Nested and epithelioid melanocytes with occasional pagetoid upward migration. Atypical cells exhibit pleomorphism, high nuclear to cytoplasmic ratios, and cherry-red nucleoli. Diagnosis: atypical deep penetrating nevus (DPN).

label: nevus
original: Histopathology of an atypical deep penetrating nevus (DPN) at 200x magnification, stained with hematoxylin and eosin. This panel shows the dermal component where melanocytes are spindled and admixed with melanophages. Scattered cells within the dermis exhibit severe cytologic atypia, consistent with the increased and more than random atypia found in atypical DPN. These cells may display features such as pleomorphism, high nuclear to cytoplasmic ratios, conspicuous cherry-red nucleoli, variable cytoplasmic melanin pigment, amphophilic cytoplasm, and rare multinucleation. In this specific case, findings on fluorescence in situ hybridization (FISH) with probes for RREB1, CCND1, MYC, and CDKN2A were within normal limits.
cleaned: Spindled melanocytes and melanophages are present in the dermal component. Scattered cells within the dermis exhibit severe cytologic atypia with pleomorphism, high nuclear to cytoplasmic ratios, conspicuous red nucleoli, variable cytoplasmic melanin pigment, and amphophilic cytoplasm; rare multinucleation is observed. Atypical deep penetrating nevus.

label: nevus
original: Histopathology of sentinel lymph node deposits from a patient with an atypical deep penetrating nevus, stained with Hematoxylin and eosin at 40× magnification. Atypical deep penetrating nevi may show nodal deposits on sentinel lymph node biopsy.
cleaned: Nodal deposits with atypical melanocytes exhibiting pleomorphism and hyperchromatic nuclei. Diagnosis: Atypical deep penetrating nevus.

label: nevus
original: Histopathology of sentinel lymph node deposits from a patient with an atypical deep penetrating nevus, stained with Hematoxylin and eosin at 200× magnification. Multiple clusters of coarsely and densely pigmented epithelioid cells are noted within the lymph node parenchyma and subcapsular space. Atypical deep penetrating nevi may show nodal deposits on sentinel lymph node biopsy.
cleaned: Clusters of coarsely and densely pigmented epithelioid cells are noted within the lymph node parenchyma and subcapsular space. Atypical deep penetrating nevus.

label: melanoma
original: Low magnification histopathology showing an ovoidal-shaped, poorly-pigmented choroidal tumor with sharply circumscribed margins. This tumor is located in the posterior ocular chamber, protruding into the posterior segment of the eye, and is consistent with a 'balloon cell melanoma of the choroid'. The surgical sample was fixed in neutral-buffered 10% formalin and submitted for histologic examination. Staining is hematoxylin and eosin (original magnification 25×).
cleaned: Ovoidal-shaped, poorly-pigmented tumor with sharply circumscribed margins protruding into the posterior segment of the eye. Balloon cell melanoma of the choroid.

label: melanoma
original: Low magnification histopathology showing that the choroidal tumor, due to its mass effect, induces a massive retinal detachment in the posterior segment of the eye. This feature is consistent with a 'balloon cell melanoma of the choroid'. Staining is hematoxylin and eosin (original magnification 50×).
cleaned: Mass effect inducing retinal detachment in the posterior segment of the eye. Tumor composed of balloon cells. Diagnosis: balloon cell melanoma of the choroid.

label: melanoma
original: High magnification histopathology of the choroidal tumor, exhibiting a diffuse ballooning morphology. The tumor is mainly composed (90%) of small- to medium-sized, ovoidal cells with rounded to oval-shaped hyperchromatic nuclei, dispersed chromatin, inconspicuous nucleoli, and abundant clear or weakly eosinophilic granular cytoplasm. Few inflammatory cells, including lymphocytes and melanin-laden macrophages, are admixed with the neoplastic cells. These morphological features support the diagnosis of 'balloon cell melanoma of the choroid'. Staining is hematoxylin and eosin (original magnification 400×).
cleaned: Diffuse ballooning morphology with small- to medium-sized, ovoidal cells containing rounded to oval-shaped hyperchromatic nuclei, dispersed chromatin, inconspicuous nucleoli, and abundant clear or weakly eosinophilic granular cytoplasm. Few lymphocytes and melanin-laden macrophages are admixed. Balloon cell melanoma of the choroid.

label: melanoma
original: Histopathology showing a small portion (10%) of the choroidal neoplasm composed of more pigmented spindle-shaped cells. These neoplastic cells have elongated nuclei and conspicuous (evident) nucleoli, representing a conventional spindle-cell morphology, which is a component of 'balloon cell melanoma of the choroid'. Staining is hematoxylin and eosin (original magnification 150×).
cleaned: Spindle-shaped cells with elongated nuclei and conspicuous nucleoli. Pigmented cells are present. Architecture consistent with balloon cell melanoma of the choroid.

label: melanoma
original: Photomicrograph of balloon cell melanoma from the first case (female, 76 years old), showing the pseudolipoblastic/balloon cell aspects of the melanocytes, characterized by a swollen histological appearance and the disintegration of disordered and abundant melanic pigment. The lesion consists of more than 50% “balloon-shaped” melanocytes, featuring abundant and finely vacuolized cytoplasm and hyperchromatic nuclei generally located in the periphery of the cell, but not pycnotic. Melanin is quantitatively reduced within the cell, with a disordered dispersion within the lesion itself and in numerous melanophages. Architecturally, the cells are organized in large pale masses that replace the dermis and thin the epidermis, forming large solid sheets of “ballooniform” melanocytes divided into irregular aggregates by thin collagenous septa, with no clear signs of activity at the dermo-epidermal junction or pagetoid spreading. Very rare mitoses were observed. Hematoxylin-Eosin stain, Original Magnification 4×.
cleaned:
Melanocytes with swollen histological appearance and disintegrated, disordered melanic pigment. Lesion consists of more than 50% “balloon-shaped” melanocytes, featuring abundant, finely vacuolized cytoplasm and hyperchromatic nuclei located peripherally. Reduced melanin within cells, with disordered dispersion and numerous melanophages. Cells organized in large pale masses replacing the dermis and thinning the epidermis, forming solid sheets divided into irregular aggregates by thin collagenous septa. 

Diagnosis: Balloon cell melanoma.

label: melanoma
original: Histological micrograph of balloon cell melanoma from patient number two (male, 75 years old), showing two different neoplastic parts: on the right, the characteristic “balloon cell” part, while in the center and on the left, a component of spindle cells of malignant melanoma is present. Additionally, in this case, there was an abundant and irregular presence of melanic pigment. The lesion consists of more than 50% “balloon-shaped” melanocytes, featuring abundant and finely vacuolized cytoplasm and hyperchromatic nuclei generally located in the periphery of the cell, but not pycnotic. Melanin is quantitatively reduced within the cell, with a disordered dispersion within the lesion itself and in numerous melanophages. Architecturally, the cells are organized in large pale masses that replace the dermis and thin the epidermis, forming large solid sheets of “ballooniform” melanocytes divided into irregular aggregates by thin collagenous septa, with no clear signs of activity at the dermo-epidermal junction or pagetoid spreading. Very rare mitoses were observed. Hematoxylin-Eosin stain, Original Magnification 4×.
cleaned:
Large pale masses of atypical melanocytes replace the dermis and thin the epidermis. The lesion contains both “balloon cells” with abundant, finely vacuolized cytoplasm and hyperchromatic, peripherally located nuclei, and spindle-shaped cells. Irregularly dispersed melanic pigment is present, with numerous melanophages. Cells are organized in solid sheets divided by thin collagenous septa. 

Diagnosis: Balloon cell melanoma.

label: melanoma
original: Histological preparation of sections from balloon cell melanoma in the third patient (female, 36 years old), displaying both balloon cell morphology and potentially a spindle cell component similar to what was observed in case number two. The lesion consists of more than 50% “balloon-shaped” melanocytes, featuring abundant and finely vacuolized cytoplasm and hyperchromatic nuclei generally located in the periphery of the cell, but not pycnotic. Melanin is quantitatively reduced within the cell, with a disordered dispersion within the lesion itself and in numerous melanophages. Architecturally, the cells are organized in large pale masses that replace the dermis and thin the epidermis, forming large solid sheets of “ballooniform” melanocytes divided into irregular aggregates by thin collagenous septa, with no clear signs of activity at the dermo-epidermal junction or pagetoid spreading. Very rare mitoses were observed. Hematoxylin-Eosin stain, Original Magnification: 10×.
cleaned: Large pale masses of “balloon-shaped” melanocytes with abundant, finely vacuolized cytoplasm and hyperchromatic, peripherally located nuclei replace the dermis and thin the epidermis. Cells are organized in solid sheets divided by thin collagenous septa. Reduced melanin is present with disordered dispersion and numerous melanophages. Balloon cell melanoma.

label: melanoma
original: Photomicrograph of balloon cell melanoma of the lesion in patient number four (male, 51 years old), displaying the characteristic balloon-shaped appearance of melanocytes with histological features that sometimes resemble pseudolipoblasts. The lesion consists of more than 50% “balloon-shaped” melanocytes, which specifically feature an abundant and finely vacuolized cytoplasm and hyperchromatic nuclei generally located in the periphery of the cell, but not pycnotic. Very rare mitoses are observed, and melanin is quantitatively reduced within the cell, with a disordered dispersion within the lesion itself and in numerous melanophages. Architecturally, the cells are organized in large pale masses that replace the dermis and thin the epidermis, forming large solid sheets of “ballooniform” melanocytes divided into irregular aggregates by thin collagenous septa, with no clear signs of activity at the dermo-epidermal junction or pagetoid spreading. Hematoxylin-Eosin stain, Original Magnification: 20×.
cleaned: Balloon cell melanoma displaying balloon-shaped melanocytes with abundant, finely vacuolized cytoplasm and hyperchromatic, peripherally located nuclei. Rare mitoses are observed. Reduced melanin is present with disordered dispersion and numerous melanophages. Large pale masses of cells replace the dermis and thin the epidermis, forming solid sheets divided into irregular aggregates by thin collagenous septa. No clear activity at the dermo-epidermal junction or pagetoid spreading is seen.

label: melanoma
original: Histological micrograph showing the cytological detail of melanocytes with balloon cell characteristics of the cytoplasm, from a patient with balloon cell melanoma. The lesion consists of more than 50% “balloon-shaped” melanocytes, featuring an abundant and finely vacuolized cytoplasm and hyperchromatic nuclei generally located in the periphery of the cell, but not pycnotic. Melanin is quantitatively reduced within the cell, with a disordered dispersion within the lesion itself and in numerous melanophages. Architecturally, the cells are organized in large pale masses that replace the dermis and thin the epidermis, forming large solid sheets of “ballooniform” melanocytes divided into irregular aggregates by thin collagenous septa, with no clear signs of activity at the dermo-epidermal junction or pagetoid spreading. Very rare mitoses were observed. Hematoxylin-Eosin stain, Original Magnification: 40×.
cleaned: Melanocytes with abundant, finely vacuolized cytoplasm and hyperchromatic, peripherally located nuclei. Reduced melanin within cells, with disordered dispersion and numerous melanophages. Large, pale masses of cells replace the dermis and thin the epidermis, forming solid sheets divided by thin collagenous septa. No clear activity at the dermo-epidermal junction or pagetoid spreading. Balloon cell melanoma.

label: melanoma
original: Histopathological analysis of uveal melanoma stained with haematoxylin-eosin.
cleaned: Spindle and epithelioid cells with abundant melanin pigment arranged in fascicles and nests. Tumor cells exhibit marked pleomorphism and hyperchromatic nuclei. Uveal melanoma.

label: melanoma
original: Histopathology showing submucosal melanoma (blue star). The biopsy confirmed a malignant proliferation of atypical pleomorphic cells with cherry-red macronuclei and amphophilic cytoplasm containing pigment, ultimately diagnosed as malignant melanoma, further confirmed by positive Sox10 and S100 staining. The image is stained with Hematoxylin and Eosin (H&E) at × 200 magnification.
cleaned: Malignant proliferation of atypical pleomorphic cells with cherry-red macronuclei and amphophilic cytoplasm containing pigment within the submucosa. Malignant melanoma.

label: melanoma
original: Histopathology showing the proliferation of markedly atypical melanocytes with prominent macronuclei (blue arrow) and cytoplasmic pigment (red arrow). These cells are part of a malignant proliferation of atypical pleomorphic cells, characterized by cherry-red macronuclei and amphophilic cytoplasm containing pigment, confirming the diagnosis of malignant melanoma, which was also positive for Sox10 and S100. The image is stained with Hematoxylin and Eosin (H&E) at × 400 magnification.
cleaned: Proliferation of markedly atypical melanocytes with prominent macronuclei and cytoplasmic pigment. A malignant proliferation of atypical pleomorphic cells, characterized by cherry-red macronuclei and amphophilic cytoplasm containing pigment. Malignant melanoma.

label: melanoma
original: Histopathology of the anorectal tumor from a 63-year-old woman who underwent surgical resection, revealing anorectal malignant melanoma. The image shows smaller tumor cells with a diffuse distribution. Stained with hematoxylin-eosin.
cleaned: Malignant melanoma. Smaller tumor cells with a diffuse distribution.

label: melanoma
original: Histopathological examination (hematoxylin–eosin stain) of the metastatic tumor from a modified radical mastectomy in the outer upper quadrant of the right breast. The morphological findings are consistent with metastatic malignant melanoma, which was histologically confirmed and is believed to have originated from the anorectal region. Core needle biopsy of the suspicious mass in the right breast had previously revealed a malignant tumor.
cleaned: Metastatic tumor with malignant melanoma morphology. Darkly pigmented atypical melanocytes are present. Tissue architecture is disrupted by nests of tumor cells.

label: melanoma
original: Histopathology showing dermal melanoma, epithelioid subtype, from a 77-year-old male patient who presented with a giant oozing, necrotic tumor on the scalp. Histological analysis of a biopsy sample (collected before the tumor reached nodular shape) revealed BRAF-mutant melanoma with isolated neoplastic infiltration under the epidermis, lacking an epidermal component and evidence of regression. Breslow thickness and Clark level could not be assessed. The tumor exhibits a high proliferation index of Ki-67 80% and non-brisk tumor-infiltrating lymphocytes (TILs). Epithelioid polygonal cells show mitotic activity of 15/1 mm², and the neoplastic mass has focal necrosis. (hematoxylin–eosin staining, magnification 50-fold).
cleaned: Dermal melanoma, epithelioid subtype. Neoplastic infiltration under the epidermis, lacking an epidermal component. Epithelioid polygonal cells with mitotic activity and focal necrosis.

label: melanoma
original: Histopathology showing nodular melanoma (NM), epithelioid subtype, from a 23-year-old male patient who was admitted with a large (5 × 4 cm), firm, black, necrotic tumor on the parietal scalp. The histopathologic report indicated a Breslow thickness of 12 mm and Clark level V, with the presence of ulceration and microsatellites. (hematoxylin–eosin staining, magnification 50-fold).
cleaned: Nodular melanoma with epithelioid cells. The lesion displays a nodular architecture with areas of necrosis. Cells exhibit pleomorphism and prominent nucleoli.

label: melanoma
original: Histopathology of nodular melanoma (NM) from the same 23-year-old male patient described in panel C. The image demonstrates signs of neuro-invasion, where melanoma cells infiltrated dermal nerve fibers, and angio-invasion, with clusters of melanoma cells found in the lumen of small blood vessels in the tumor front. (hematoxylin–eosin staining, magnification 280-fold).
cleaned: Nodular melanoma with infiltrating atypical melanocytes extending into dermal nerve fibers and small blood vessels at the tumor front. Clusters of atypical cells are visible within vessel lumens.

label: melanoma
original: Histopathology image of a nodular melanoma (NM) biopsy, showing significant deposits of melanin within some of the tumor cells, which is a significant feature for this tumor type. The primary tumor was composed of atypical epithelioid cells and also exhibited strong immune positivity for SOX10, HMB-45, and Melan-A. (hematoxylin–eosin staining, magnification 130-fold).
cleaned: Nodular melanoma showing deposits of melanin within tumor cells. Tumor composed of atypical epithelioid cells.

label: melanoma
original: Histopathology image showing nodular melanoma (NM) with prominent nuclear inclusions in atypical epithelioid cells. The primary tumor biopsy for this case also presented strong immune positivity for SOX10, HMB-45, and Melan-A. (hematoxylin–eosin staining, magnification 380-fold).
cleaned: Nodular melanoma with prominent nuclear inclusions in atypical epithelioid cells.

label: melanoma
original: Histopathology image of nodular melanoma (NM), epithelioid subtype, from a 67-year-old male patient who presented with a rapidly growing, bleeding melanoma tumor measuring 5 cm in the right heel region. The tumor has a Breslow depth of 10 mm and is Clark level V, with the presence of microsatellites, absent tumor-infiltrating lymphocytes (TILs), and a radial growth pattern. There is no evidence of angio- or neuro-invasions. (hematoxylin–eosin staining, magnification 30-fold).
cleaned: Nodular melanoma with epithelioid cells. The lesion demonstrates a radial growth pattern and microsatellites. Tumor cells are present within the dermis.

label: melanoma
original: Histopathology (H&E stain) revealing melanoma in situ, lentigo maligna type, on shave biopsy from the 5 cm ovoid pink-white plaque on the arm of a 69-year-old female patient. The patient had a five-year history of an enlarging lesion that was unresponsive to previous treatments for tinea corporis, nummular dermatitis, and actinic keratosis. Microscopy is performed at 100x total magnification.
cleaned: Melanoma in situ, lentigo maligna type. Oval-shaped lesion with pink-white coloration. Increased atypical melanocytes present within the epidermis.

label: melanoma
original: Histopathology (H&E stain) revealing invasive lentigo maligna melanoma with a Breslow depth of 0.3 mm. This finding is associated with the 5 cm ovoid pink-white plaque on the arm of a 69-year-old female patient, who presented with an asymptomatic growth that had enlarged over five years and was unresponsive to various prior treatments. Microscopy is performed at 100x total magnification.
cleaned: Invasive lentigo maligna melanoma. Ovoid lesion with pink-white coloration. Irregular nests of atypical melanocytes infiltrating the tissue. Variable cell morphology with prominent nucleoli.

label: nevus
original: Histopathology of nevus pathology. A low-power view shows two foci of pigmented cells within a collagenous stroma in the superficial dermis, which is suggestive of a benign blue nevus. The image is stained with hematoxylin and eosin; original magnification: ×10.
cleaned: Two foci of pigmented cells within a collagenous stroma in the superficial dermis. Diagnosis: nevus.

label: nevus
original: Histopathology of nevus pathology. A high-power view reveals two distinct cell types: spindled-to-dendritic melanocytes (indicated by a blue arrow) and melanophages (indicated by a red arrow). The image is stained with hematoxylin and eosin; original magnification: ×40.
cleaned:
Spindled-to-dendritic melanocytes and melanophages are visible.
Nevus.

label: nevus
original: Histopathological examination showing solitary nodules, representing dermal separated nodes of a relapsed cell-rich blue nevus, located in close vicinity to the scar. The nodules are constituted by a nodular dermal proliferation of ovoid melanocytes with monomorphous nuclei and inconspicuous cytoplasm, intermingled with dendritic pigmented melanocytes. Nevus cells of the nodules are dispersed in the dermis and hyperpigmented, spindle-shaped melanocytes infiltrate among the collagen bundles. No features suggestive of malignancy, such as cytological atypia, atypical mitoses, or necrosis, are observed. Stained with Hematoxylin and Eosin (H&E) at 40× magnification. Inset shows a higher magnification view (H&E, 400×).
cleaned:
Nodules of melanocytes in the dermis, adjacent to scar tissue. Nodules display a proliferation of ovoid melanocytes with monomorphous nuclei and inconspicuous cytoplasm, alongside dendritic pigmented melanocytes. Spindle-shaped, hyperpigmented melanocytes infiltrate between collagen bundles. 

Blue nevus.

label: nevus
original: Histopathological examination showing recurrent cellular blue cell nevus (also referred to as relapsed cellular blue nevus). The image displays solitary nodules in close vicinity to a scar, constituted by a nodular dermal proliferation of ovoid melanocytes with monomorphous nuclei and inconspicuous cytoplasm, intermingled with dendritic pigmented melanocytes. Strongly pigmented oval-shaped, spindle-shaped, and dendritic melanocytes and melanophages occupy the upper and mid dermis. Nevus cells are dispersed in the dermis, with hyperpigmented, spindle-shaped melanocytes infiltrating among the collagen bundles. No features suggestive of malignancy, such as cytological atypia, atypical mitoses, or necrosis, are present. Stained with Hematoxylin and Eosin (H&E), 200× magnification.
cleaned: Nodular dermal proliferation of ovoid melanocytes with monomorphous nuclei and inconspicuous cytoplasm, intermingled with dendritic pigmented melanocytes. Strongly pigmented oval-shaped, spindle-shaped, and dendritic melanocytes and melanophages occupy the upper and mid dermis. Spindle-shaped melanocytes infiltrate among collagen bundles. Cellular blue nevus.

label: nevus
original: Hematoxylin-eosin staining of the right buccal mucosa from the proband’s father, revealing histological features consistent with White Spongy Nevus (WSN). Panel (a) shows enlarged spinous cells and well-differentiated basal cells with an increased number of layers, as indicated by the red frame. These findings, including the significant thickening of the epithelium and hyperplasia of well-differentiated basal cells, conform to the conventional pathological manifestations of WSN. Original magnification: 40×.
cleaned: Enlarged spinous cells and well-differentiated basal cells with increased layers, significant thickening of the epithelium, and hyperplasia of well-differentiated basal cells. White Spongy Nevus.

label: nevus
original: Hematoxylin-eosin staining of the right buccal mucosa from the proband’s father, revealing histological features consistent with White Spongy Nevus (WSN). Panel (b) illustrates incomplete keratinization of surface cells and uneven epithelial tissue with no inflammatory infiltration, as indicated by the red frame. These characteristics, particularly incomplete keratinization of surface cells and the absence of significant inflammatory infiltration, are key pathological findings in WSN. Original magnification: 100×.

Incomplete keratinization of surface cells and uneven epithelial tissue with no inflammatory infiltration.

original: Hematoxylin-eosin staining of the right buccal mucosa from the proband’s father, revealing histological features consistent with White Spongy Nevus (WSN). Panel (c) highlights vacuolar change of spinous cells, indicated by the red arrow. The presence of enlarged and vacuolated spiny cells is a characteristic pathological manifestation of WSN. Original magnification: 200×.
cleaned: Vacuolar change of spinous cells. Enlarged and vacuolated spiny cells. White Spongy Nevus.

label: nevus
original: Hematoxylin-eosin staining of the right buccal mucosa from the proband’s father, revealing histological features consistent with White Spongy Nevus (WSN). Panel (d) shows spinous cells with nuclear pyknosis (top arrow) and cell edema (bottom arrow). These findings, including nuclei consolidation and edematous spiny cells, are characteristic pathological manifestations of WSN. Original magnification: 400×.
cleaned: Spinous cells with condensed nuclei and cell edema. Nuclei consolidation and edematous spiny cells are visible within the tissue architecture. Diagnosis: White Spongy Nevus.
